Title: Business & Data Analyst
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
¿Qué estamos buscando?




Actualmente nos encontramos en búsqueda de nuestro/nuestra futuro/futura Business Consultant & Data Analyst cuya misión es diseñar, analizar, controlar y optimizar los procesos operativos de las áreas bajo su responsabilidad dentro de la Dirección de Operaciones, aportando inteligencia de negocio y visión analítica para garantizar la eficiencia, la calidad del servicio y la escalabilidad de los procesos.




La persona seleccionada será responsable del análisis de datos operativos, el seguimiento de KPIs, cuadros de mando y SLA’s, con el objetivo de detectar desviaciones, anticipar tendencias y proponer mejoras continuas que contribuyan al cumplimiento de los objetivos operativos y presupuestarios de la compañía.




Asimismo, velará para que los procesos operativos sean robustos, eficientes, alineados con la normativa vigente y con los estándares de calidad establecidos, asegurando una correcta ejecución tanto por equipos internos como externos.







Hablemos del Proyecto...




Como Business Consultant & Data Analyst tu misión será:




Analizar la viabilidad de nuevas tareas o procesos dentro de la Unidad de Operaciones, elaborando informes en los que se recojan las necesidades, el impacto y el coste/esfuerzo asociado.
Analizar el comportamiento de los distintos servicios mediante datos, cuadros de mando e indicadores operativos para identificar oportunidades de mejora y anticipar posibles desviaciones.
Diseñar y documentar los procesos operativos, definiendo los flujos detallados y asegurando el cumplimiento normativo con el menor coste posible para la compañía.
Asegurar la correcta ejecución de los procesos por parte de personal interno y/o externo, garantizando la alineación con el modelo operativo definido.
Realizar el seguimiento de los KPIs operativos y promover acciones correctoras en caso de detectar desviaciones o ineficiencias.
Supervisar las auditorías de calidad y el cumplimiento de los niveles de servicio y estándares establecidos.
Mantener una interlocución continua con las áreas de negocio para la revisión de necesidades, definición de planes de acción y resolución de incidencias operativas.







¿Qué valoramos de tu candidatura?




Posees Licenciatura o Grado en Administración y Dirección de Empresas, Economía, Ingeniería, Matemáticas, Estadística o similar. Se valorará formación complementaria en análisis de datos, gestión de procesos, mejora continua (Lean, Six Sigma) u otras metodologías afines.
Tienes experiencia mínima de 3 años en posiciones similares en áreas de Operaciones, Procesos, Control de Gestión o Analítica. Experiencia demostrable en diseño, definición y optimización de procesos operativos.
Posees experiencia en el seguimiento de KPIs, elaboración de cuadros de mando y control de SLA’s. Valorable experiencia en entornos financieros, financieros no bancarios o de servicios.







¿Qué te ofrecemos?




Serás parte de un equipo de profesionales de primer nivel, con una excelente reputación en el sector.
Podrás disfrutar de una amplia oferta formativa.
Aprenderás de la mano de Managers de referencia de las distintas direcciones.
Ampliarás tu red de contactos a través de la colaboración y espacios de networking.
Contarás con opciones de desarrollo de carrera y movilidad dentro del Grupo. Somos un grupo bancario que sienta sus bases en los principios de meritocracia y el desarrollo de sus colaboradores.
Contratación indefinida y remuneración competitiva.







Banco Sabadell está comprometido en promover ambientes de trabajo en los que se trate con respeto y dignidad a las personas, procurando el desarrollo profesional de la plantilla y garantizando la igualdad de oportunidades en su selección, formación y promoción ofreciendo un entorno de trabajo libre de cualquier discriminación por motivo de género, edad, orientación sexual, religión, etnia o cualquier otra circunstancia personal o social.




Banco Sabadell forma parte de la red de empresas con el distintivo de “Igualdad en la Empresa” otorgado por el Ministerio de Igualdad.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
En Volkswagen Group Services Barcelona estamos buscando un/ nuevo/a Compañero/a que dará soporte directo en análisis de datos de negocio al responsable de área. Proporcionará información estructurada, informes periódicos e insights relevantes sobre el rendimiento de los servicios. Su labor se centrará en el análisis de indicadores clave como enrollment, automatic service scheduling y la evolución de los servicios por mercado, país y modelo, así como en el mantenimiento de dashboards y herramientas de reporting en Power BI, asegurando la calidad, fiabilidad y disponibilidad de la información necesaria para la toma de decisiones del área.

Entre las funciones del puesto:

1. Soporte en el análisis de datos del servicio: Dar apoyo al responsable interno en el análisis de datos de servicios conectados (enrollment, automatic service scheduling, métricas de negocio adicionales, uso por mercado/modelo), facilitando la identificación de patrones y desviaciones relevantes.

2.Apoyo en la generación de insights accionables: Asistir al responsable interno en la extracción de conclusiones relevantes a partir de los datos, enfocadas en mejorar el negocio y la experiencia del cliente.

3. Apoyo en la definición y seguimiento de KPIs: Colaborar en la definición, mantenimiento y actualización de KPIs de segundo nivel orientados a negocio, asegurando consistencia de métricas.

4. Preparación de información para la toma de decisiones: Preparar y estructurar datos, informes y resúmenes ejecutivos como base para las decisiones del responsable del área.

5. Elaboración de informes periódicos y análisis puntuales: Ejecutar reportes recurrentes y análisis ad-hoc bajo demanda del responsable interno o de otros stakeholders del área.

6. Soporte en la gestión de Power BI y reporting: Mantener dashboards, modelos de datos y visualizaciones en Power BI bajo la dirección del responsable del área.

7. Apoyo en el control de calidad del dato: Colaborar en la revisión de fuentes de datos, identificación de inconsistencias y validación de resultados de reporting.

8. Coordinación con equipos técnicos y de datos: Alinear fuentes, definiciones de KPI y metodología con IT y equipos analíticos.

9. Preparación de presentaciones y documentación: Elaborar materiales de soporte para comités, reuniones y comunicaciones internas.




Qué esperamos de ti:

Grado universitario en Ingeniería Economía, Analítica de Datos o formación equivalente.
Valorable formación específica en gestión de proyectos data analytics o negocios digitales.
Valorable formación en Power BI y Business Intelligence (modelado de datos, DAX, visualización y gobierno del dato).
Análisis de datos, reporting avanzado o herramientas BI complementarias.
Mínimo 2 años en funciones de soporte analítico, reporting, gestión de datos o soporte operativo a áreas de negocio, preferiblemente en entornos digitales, tecnológicos o de automoción.
Inglés c1
Análisis e interpretación de datos orientados a negocio.
Definición y seguimiento de KPIs y métricas de rendimiento.
Gestión administrativa y presupuestaria básica (volúmenes, facturación, licencias).
Capacidad para estructurar información compleja y convertirla en reportes ejecutivos.
Coordinación en entornos transversales y trabajo colaborativo con múltiples áreas.
Ofimática avanzada: PowerPoint, Excel.
Business Intelligence y reporting: Power BI (nivel medio).
Gestión de trabajo y documentación: Confluence, Jira (valorable)
Conocimiento de metodologías de trabajo Agile (SAFe, Scrum)




Qué podemos ofrecerte:

Subvención diaria para la cantina.
Acceso gratuito a transporte de empresa.
Opción para participar en el Plan de Retribución Flexible, según la normativa interna de la empresa.
Acceso subvencionado a formación en idiomas.
Acceso a Plataforma de Formación E-learning.
Beneficios corporativos.
Descuentos en automóviles del grupo.
Hasta el 60% de teletrabajo

Title: Data Analyst TRAINEE
Description:
Job Summary

We are looking for a Data Analyst Intern with 1–2 years of prior experience in data analytics, data management, or business analytics. He or she will work closely with cross-functional stakeholders (Data Engineering, Cybersecurity, IT, Business Domains, Technological Centers) and support key initiatives to drive data governance, data quality, standardize definitions, and data trust.




Key Accountabilities

Data Quality

•Assist in identifying and documenting data quality framework, ensuring correct tracking, classification, and follow-up.

•Help maintain standardized definitions, taxonomies, and controlled vocabularies across domains.

Data Catalog Management

•Contribute to promote the adoption and awareness of the company’s Data Catalog.

•Update glossary terms and domain ownership and information management.

Collaboration & Enablement

• Support Data & Digital Innovation to document and communicate data governance processes

• Document and manage content in different formats for presentations and communication strategy. Collaborate with other Data and business teams to understand data issues and support resolution.

•Contribute to training materials, onboarding guides, and communication updates.




Minimum Knowledge & Experience required for the position:

•Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Information Systems, Engineering, Computer Science, Statistics, Business Analytics, or related discipline (completed or in final stage).

•1–2 years of experience (through full-time roles, internships, or academic projects) in Data analysis, data management, or business intelligence.




Skills & Capabilities:

Basic understanding of:

•Data governance and or Data management tools & concepts (data lineage, metadata, data quality, RBAC, data privacy).

•SQL and/or Python

•Data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, Qlik).

Preferred Skills

•Cloud Data Platforms knowledge (Azure, AWS, GCP) and modern data architectures.

•Strong communication and documentation skills. Writen and verbal english communication skills

•Detail-oriented mindset and curiosity for understanding data end to end

•Ability to collaborate in a multi-disciplinary environment and complex organization matrix

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About BizAway

Here at BizAway, we Deliver the Future of Travel. 😎

We are a solid international company with strong ambitions and great expertise. With a focus on sustainability 🌱, on a daily basis we support companies enabling them to improve their travel management through our constantly evolving services and solutions, always characterized by our tech attitude and smart and innovative processes. 🚀

We know that success comes from People and deserves to be recognized.👫Proactivity and

Reliability, Kind collaboration, and communication are the core values of our Solution Culture.

If you like challenges and would love to be part of one of the fastest-growing B2B scale-ups, then BizAway is the company you have been looking for. 🤩

We are looking for

We are looking for a driven and enthusiastic Data Analyst (m/w/d) , who desires to contribute to the growth and success of our Data & Analytics Team.

Your role in BizAway


You manage and improve our current reporting system, supporting all internal needs for Data visualization of data findings through charts, graphs, and dashboards to make our data understandable.
You learn all aspects of the travel industry from Finance, Products, Sales and Service/Operations perspective, translating data into actionable insights resulting in verifiable reporting.
By having domain expertise in our industry, you engage with Business and Strategy stakeholders to help define KPIs, including how data will be captured, shared, and leveraged across our departments.
You contribute to creating the base foundations of a comprehensive source of truth through data modeling actions


Your Basic Qualifications


Previously worked in a Tech Company
Demonstrated experience with Product Analytics tools (eg: Amplitude, Mixpanel, Pendo, Google Analytics)
Demonstrated experience with BI Data Visualization tools (Qlik preferred)
Data analysis, presentation, and visualization experience
Strong communication skills required
Ability to work alone or in a team as required
Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Engineering, Economics or a related field is preferred
English proficiency (written and spoken level B2)


We are also interested in


Knowledge of programming languages such as Python
Experience with ETL tools
Understanding of AI/ML concepts


Our offer

📄 Contract type: Permanent

🕓 Working hours: Full-time – 40h/week, from Monday to Friday

🏢 Hybrid setting, flexible working policy, and great offices in Europe

💰 Salary: Attractive compensation, including equity in the company

💼 Equity in the company (stock options plan)

✈️ A seat on a scale-up with skyrocketing growth

📈 Development of your entrepreneurial spirit, having the chance to implement real-impact business decision

🌍Multicultural and international team

🙌Collaborative and smart environment to work and learn

🍻Free coffee to kick-start your day and free beers to celebrate together

BizAway avoids any discrimination based on age, gender, sexual orientation, health status, nationality, political opinions, and religious beliefs in all decisions affecting personnel selection.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
En Zazume estamos transformando la forma en la que las personas alquilan sus viviendas. Nuestra misión es ayudar a propietarios e inquilinos a gestionar el alquiler de forma segura, transparente y eficiente a través de nuestra plataforma tecnológica. Zazume ofrece herramientas que cubren desde la búsqueda y precualificación de inquilinos hasta la gestión integral del alquiler y el cobro de rentas garantizadas, todo en un entorno totalmente digital.




Al contrario de las agencias inmobiliarias tradicionales, combinamos tecnología, datos y atención personalizada para que nuestros usuarios tengan control y visibilidad en cada paso del alquiler. Gracias a nuestro enfoque innovador, conseguimos garantizar la tranquilidad tanto de propietarios como de inquilinos, reduciendo fricciones, impagos y tareas administrativas.




Nos encontramos en un momento de crecimiento y queremos seguir construyendo un equipo ambicioso, con ganas de aprender y generar impacto real en el negocio.




Buscamos un/a Data Analyst con mentalidad de negocio, capaz de transformar datos en decisiones accionables.




Este rol es clave para conectar datos, producto y negocio. Trabajarás con información compleja (mercado inmobiliario, pricing, riesgo, comportamiento de usuarios…) para entender qué está pasando, por qué, y qué deberíamos hacer al respecto.




No buscamos solo alguien que “haga dashboards”, sino alguien que haga las preguntas correctas y genere impacto real.




¿Qué harás en tu día a día?





Crear análisis y visualizaciones que apoyen decisiones de negocio y producto.


Explorar, limpiar y analizar datasets complejos de diferentes fuentes.


Traducir preguntas de negocio en análisis concretos.


Definir y hacer seguimiento de KPIs clave.


Asegurar la calidad, consistencia y trazabilidad de los datos.


Comunicar resultados de forma clara y convincente a stakeholders no técnicos.





¿Qué necesitamos de ti?





Experiencia previa en Data Analysis (idealmente en entornos dinámicos o startups).


Dominio de SQL (imprescindible).


Experiencia con herramientas de BI (Metabase, Looker, Tableau, Power BI…).


Formación en negocio con postgrado/máster en Data Analytics o Big Data (o perfil inverso: formación técnica en datos con sólida visión de negocio)


Buen manejo de Python o Excel avanzado para análisis.


Fuerte pensamiento analítico y orientación a negocio. Capacidad para estructurar problemas y priorizar.


Habilidad para comunicar insights de forma clara y accionable.


Conocimiento de métricas de negocio (funnels, cohortes, unit economics…).


Valorable conocimientos de estadística y conocimientos básicos de IA generativa (aunque no es el foco principal).

Contrato fijo indefinido y teletrabajo flexible (1-2 días presencial en oficina)


Oportunidad de colaboración con todas las áreas del negocio: Sales, Operations, Finance, Marketing, Producto, etc…


Entorno de alto aprendizaje con datasets reales, retos variados y mucho ownership.


Oficinas en el centro Barcelona: buen acceso con tren y metro


Horario flexible y cultura basada en confianza.

23 días de vacaciones + el día de tu cumpleaños

Entrevista por videollamada con HR Manager

En Zazume estamos transformando la forma en la que las personas alquilan sus viviendas. Nuestra misión es ayudar a propietarios e inquilinos a gestionar el alquiler de forma segura, transparente y eficiente a través de nuestra plataforma tecnológica. Zazume ofrece herramientas que cubren desde la búsqueda y precualificación de inquilinos hasta la gestión integral del alquiler y el cobro de rentas garantizadas, todo en un entorno totalmente digital.


Al contrario de las agencias inmobiliarias tradicionales, combinamos tecnología, datos y atención personalizada para que nuestros usuarios tengan control y visibilidad en cada paso del alquiler. Gracias a nuestro enfoque innovador, conseguimos garantizar la tranquilidad tanto de propietarios como de inquilinos, reduciendo fricciones, impagos y tareas administrativas.


Nos encontramos en un momento de crecimiento y queremos seguir construyendo un equipo ambicioso, con ganas de aprender y generar impacto real en el negocio.


Buscamos un/a Data Analyst con mentalidad de negocio, capaz de transformar datos en decisiones accionables.


Este rol es clave para conectar datos, producto y negocio. Trabajarás con información compleja (mercado inmobiliario, pricing, riesgo, comportamiento de usuarios…) para entender qué está pasando, por qué, y qué deberíamos hacer al respecto.


No buscamos solo alguien que “haga dashboards”, sino alguien que haga las preguntas correctas y genere impacto real.


¿Qué harás en tu día a día?



  • Crear análisis y visualizaciones que apoyen decisiones de negocio y producto.

  • Explorar, limpiar y analizar datasets complejos de diferentes fuentes.

  • Traducir preguntas de negocio en análisis concretos.

  • Definir y hacer seguimiento de KPIs clave.

  • Asegurar la calidad, consistencia y trazabilidad de los datos.

  • Comunicar resultados de forma clara y convincente a stakeholders no técnicos.


¿Qué necesitamos de ti?



  • Experiencia previa en Data Analysis (idealmente en entornos dinámicos o startups).

  • Dominio de SQL (imprescindible).

  • Experiencia con herramientas de BI (Metabase, Looker, Tableau, Power BI…).

  • Formación en negocio con postgrado/máster en Data Analytics o Big Data (o perfil inverso: formación técnica en datos con sólida visión de negocio)

  • Buen manejo de Python o Excel avanzado para análisis.

  • Fuerte pensamiento analítico y orientación a negocio. Capacidad para estructurar problemas y priorizar.

  • Habilidad para comunicar insights de forma clara y accionable.

  • Conocimiento de métricas de negocio (funnels, cohortes, unit economics…).

  • Valorable conocimientos de estadística y conocimientos básicos de IA generativa (aunque no es el foco principal).


¿Qué ofrece Zazume?



  • Contrato fijo indefinido y teletrabajo flexible (1-2 días presencial en oficina)

  • Oportunidad de colaboración con todas las áreas del negocio: Sales, Operations, Finance, Marketing, Producto, etc…

  • Entorno de alto aprendizaje con datasets reales, retos variados y mucho ownership.

  • Oficinas en el centro Barcelona: buen acceso con tren y metro

  • Horario flexible y cultura basada en confianza.

  • Retribución flexible (ticket restaurant + seguro médico)

  • 23 días de vacaciones + el día de tu cumpleaños


¿Cómo será el proceso de selección?



  1. Entrevista por videollamada con HR Manager

  2. Entrevista presencial con CTO + Head of Finance

  3. Business Case / Prueba técnica


¿Te sumas al equipo?

Title: Data Analyst – Power BI
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
En BETWEEN Technology, buscamos un/a Data Analyst para unirse a nuestro equipo en Sant Cugat . Buscamos un/a Analista de Datos con al menos 2-3 años de experiencia en herramientas de análisis y visualización como Excel, Power BI, Power Query, DAX, y conocimientos en Python.

Esta posición contribuye al desarrollo de informes y análisis de datos, principalmente mediante Power BI, proporcionando soporte al equipo de Reporting. Su labor será asegurar la disponibilidad, trazabilidad y calidad de la información.

Responsabilidades Principales


Análisis de datos: Recopilar, transformar y analizar datos provenientes de diversas fuentes utilizando Excel, Power BI y Power Query, con el objetivo de generar informes para el negocio.
Modelado de datos: Diseñar y mantener modelos de datos robustos mediante DAX, que permitan realizar análisis complejos y cálculos personalizados adaptados a las necesidades de cada área.
Desarrollo de informes: Crear dashboards e informes interactivos en Power BI que faciliten la visualización clara y efectiva de los resultados, orientados a distintos niveles de la organización.
Automatización de procesos: Utilizar Python para procesamiento de datos, mejorando la eficiencia operativa y reducir errores manuales.
Cierres mensuales y reporting corporativo: Participar activamente en los cierres mensuales, asegurando la correcta consolidación de datos y elaboración de reportes que se remiten a las oficinas de Corporate, cumpliendo con los estándares de calidad y plazos establecidos.


Requisitos


Mínimo 2-3 años de experiencia en análisis y manipulación de datos con Excel.
Experiencia demostrable en el desarrollo de informes interactivos con Power BI, DAX para modelado de datos.
Experiencia en procesos de cierre mensual y reporting corporativo.
Conocimientos en Python.
Capacidad para comunicar resultados de forma clara y efectiva.
Autonomía en el trabajo y capacidad para colaborar en equipos multidisciplinares.


Requisitos valorables


Conocimiento de bases de datos: SQL, Fabrik…

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At Joko, we help consumers shop smarter. Our mission is to revolutionize shopping, empowering people to find what they need, make informed decisions, and save money.

Founded in Paris, Joko is a tech company and certified B Corp with over 90 talents across Paris, Barcelona, and New York (and beyond). More than 6 million users already save money every day at 10,000+ merchants with Joko.

From cashback and automatic coupons to price alerts and carbon tracking, we keep expanding our products to make shopping smarter. We’re now building an AI-powered shopping assistant to help users find the best products by price, quality, and impact.

Having reached profitability in our core market, we’re now scaling globally, with a strong focus on the US.

It’s still day 1, come build the future of shopping with us!

This position is remote-friendly.

🧚🏻 Our Data team

The Data team at Joko is turning mountains of data into actionable insights for all the teams! The Data team is part of our Operations department led by our COO, and our mission is to empower the company to make informed decisions.

More Specifically, The Team's Responsibilities Are


State-of-the-art analytics & data science: We want to be at the forefront of analytics and data science by building top-notch analyses and tools. Our goal is to allow teams to leverage powerful insights from advanced reporting and predictive models.
AI-driven autonomy for stakeholders: We are doubling down on the combination of AI and data to unlock a new level of autonomy for our stakeholders, seamlessly bridging data insights with the AI tools we use, built on secure and trustworthy foundations.
Spread the data culture: At Joko, we have a strong engineering and tech culture across all teams. We work hand-in-hand with our stakeholders, providing support and training on data tools, and building close relationships to ensure our solutions align perfectly with their operational needs and evolve with them over time.


🎯 What you will do

As a Data Analyst, you will have a cross-functional role and collaborate closely with teams to understand our data and develop insights.


Advanced analytics & data science: You will explore and analyze data to uncover trends, patterns, and potential issues that will support decision-making processes across all teams. You will also contribute to data science initiatives by building predictive models and applying statistics to generate deeper insights and drive measurable business impact.
Reporting and Data Visualization: You will design and create dashboards and data visualizations that empower stakeholders to explore data autonomously. Leveraging AI tools to move faster, your focus will be on what matters most: defining the right metrics, surfacing meaningful insights, and ensuring data is understood and acted upon across the company.
AI and data integration: You will actively contribute to bridging our data with the AI tools we use, ensuring insights are integrated and maintained over time.
Stakeholder collaboration: You will work hand-in-hand with stakeholders to understand their needs, collect their feedback, and evangelize data usage to help them make smarter choices and become more autonomous over time.
Continuous Learning and Improvement: As "never stop learning" is one of our values, you will grow along the way and keep up with the latest analytical methods, AI developments, and tools in our data stack.


👀 Who we're looking for


Experience: You have 2+ years of professional experience in data analysis (relevant internships can count). We hire across different seniority levels, so we encourage you to apply even if you are more experienced.
Interest in AI: You are genuinely curious about AI and its applications, and eager to leverage it in your day-to-day work to move faster and create more impact.
Mindset: You have an entrepreneurial mindset, you like challenges, you welcome feedback and you are willing to get better every day to reach excellence.
Problem-solving & Analytical skills: You love solving complex problems and analyzing data.
Coding knowledge: Mastering SQL and Python is not mandatory, as you can still learn when joining, but it's a plus.
Business acumen: You can understand the needs of your business stakeholders and get the big picture of how the business is running.
Communication: You know how to present your conclusions clearly and concisely (written and oral).
Education: You have graduated from a top-tier business or engineering school.
Languages: You are fluent in English, both written and spoken.


💎 Our perks

(Some of the benefits listed below are available to full-time positions only)

At Joko, we believe that flexibility and trust are essential. Our work environment reflects this through:


Flexible remote : If you live in Paris, you can choose to work from our office or from home with no constraints. If you live elsewhere, we can provide access to a coworking space and a coworking budget.
Work from anywhere : Want to spend a month in Italy while working? You can work from most countries in the world for up to 3 months per year.


On Top Of That, We Offer Plenty Of Perks


💸 Top-market compensation
📈 Equity for everyone with the chance to own a piece of what you build
🤖 Half-day each week dedicated to leveling up with AI by exploring new tools, iterating hard, and sharpening your skills
🌴 Yearly offsite in amazing locations and budget for team-building events & monthly in-person gatherings
💪 Contribution to your ClassPass subscription
🍼 8-week leave paid 100% for the second parent
…and much more, check the full list here!


🤝 Our hiring process


Intro call: Quick screening with the Hiring Manager or the Talent team.
Step 1 – Team interview (45 min): Conversation with two Joko team members (could include the Hiring Manager, people from the team you’d join, or colleagues from other teams).
Step 2 – Role-specific assessments
For non-tech roles: Take-home case study followed by a 45 min interview. We assess both your output and how you think in real time. The exercise will be relevant to the role (e.g. analysis, strategy, or process design).
For tech roles: Live technical interviews:
Coding interview + System design interview
For research internships, an additional round may involve analyzing and presenting a research paper
Step 3 – Leadership interview (45 min): Conversation with a SteerCo member and a Founder.
References: Up to 3 calls with former colleagues or managers.

☕ You may also be invited for coffee with team members to get a feel for our culture.

Title: Data Analyst-Power BI specialist
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
Tu Talento, Nuestra Tecnología




Imagina un lugar donde tus ideas no solo son bienvenidas, sino que son el motor que impulsa nuestra evolución. En nuestra empresa, te invitamos a ser arquitect@ de tu propio éxito, explorando proyectos innovadores y desafiando constantemente tus límites, con una propuesta de valor alrededor del Workplace (Communication Services, IT Services, Printing Services) y de la Digitalización de Procesos (IT Consulting, Application Integration, Hyperautomation), todo bajo un marco de Hybrid Infrastructure (Datacenter, Cloud, Storage, Backup)

No es solo un trabajo, es una aventura de descubrimiento y aprendizaje continuo. Aquí, cada día es una oportunidad para aprender algo nuevo, colaborar con l@s mejores y ser parte de algo realmente extraordinario.

Además, en nuestra cultura laboral, la diversidad y la inclusión son pilares fundamentales. Valoramos las voces únicas y celebramos la riqueza que aportan a nuestro equipo. Junt@s, estamos construyendo un entorno donde todos prosperamos.




Actualmente esta buscando un/a Data Analyst, especialista en Power BI para nuestra área de Finances. Tu misión será la de transformar datos complejos en insights accionables, apoyar la toma de decisiones del negocio y trabajar de forma colaborativa con equipos multidisciplinares. En este caso, es imprescindible aportar un nivel alto de inglés para interactuar con stakehoders internacionales.




Tus responsabilidades:

Diseñar, desarrollar y mantener dashboards, informes y modelos de datos en Power BI.
Analizar grandes volúmenes de datos para identificar tendencias, patrones y oportunidades.
Colaborar con stakeholders para comprender necesidades y traducirlas en soluciones analíticas eficaces.
Garantizar la calidad, consistencia y precisión de los datos.
Crear y documentar procesos ETL (Extract, Transform, Load).
Generar insights claros y recomendaciones basadas en análisis cuantitativos.
Participar en reuniones y presentaciones en inglés con equipos globales.
Automatizar procesos y mejorar continuamente las soluciones analíticas existentes.




¿Qué necesitas?

Grado en Matemáticas, Estadística, Ingeniería, Economía, Informática o área relacionada.
3+ años de experiencia en análisis de datos o roles similares.
Dominio avanzado de Power BI: DAX, Power Query, modelado de datos, optimización de performance.
Experiencia con SQL (nivel intermedio/avanzado).
Nivel de inglés alto (C1 deseable); capacidad para comunicar insights de forma clara.
Capacidad analítica sólida y orientación al detalle.
Experiencia trabajando con distintos stakeholders de negocio.




¿Qué ofrecemos?

Crecimiento profesional: Incorporación al equipo de finances de Ricoh, compartiendo experiencias y mejores prácticas con compañeros en un entorno colaborativo. Por supuesto, te ofrecemos contrato indefinido, además de un plan de formación y carrera adaptado a tus expectativas.
Equilibrio trabajo-vida: Disfruta de teletrabajo y una jornada intensiva durante más de dos meses en verano, los viernes, los pre-festivos y la tarde de tu cumpleaños, para que la celebres cómo se merece.
Excelente clima laboral: Nos encanta celebrar nuestros éxitos como una gran familia. No te pierdas nuestras fiestas de Navidad, Summer Party y otros eventos solidarios y deportivos. ¡Incluso tenemos una banda y DJ que siempre nos animan con la mejor música!
Retribución flexible: Con Ricoh Flex, puedes adaptar tu retribución a tus necesidades. Encuentra beneficios como formación, salud, transporte, restaurantes, guardería y más, además de disfrutar de descuentos en nuestro Ricoh Club.

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
What You'll Do:

As part of the Business Performance Analytics team within Revenue Operations, your main mission will be to deliver robust, intuitive, and consumable business insights to empower Criteo’s Global Commercial leaders to build and drive their commercial strategy.

You will implement a data-driven approach to uncover insights and explain business performance. This role involves working with large and complex datasets, and requires someone who is comfortable exploring data creatively to identify trends, generate insights, and build a compelling narrative that informs decision-making.

This 6-month internship role will be based out in Barcelona, and starting in April/May 2026.

Your Mission:


Provide analytical insights on Criteo’s global performance, market dynamics, and competitive landscape
Identify business growth opportunities and threats, and translate findings into actionable insights for C‑level stakeholders
Leverage external and internal data to assess the potential of clients and prospects across new and existing business



Who You Are:


Bachelor’s degree or higher in a quantitative field (Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, Business School)
Strong intellectual curiosity and analytical mindset; ability to structure and solve complex problems; solid understanding of statistical modelling
Business acumen and genuine interest in the ad tech industry
Hands-on experience with SQL for data extraction/manipulation; familiarity with Python for data analysis; experience with visualization tools such as Tableau and/or Power BI
Curiosity and experience using Generative AI solutions to enhance data exploration, automate insights, or craft analytical narratives
Strong presentation skills (including PowerPoint proficiency) and ability to translate data into clear business insights and recommendations
Excellent communication skills and fluency in English
Proactivity, a collaborative approach, and commitment



We acknowledge that many candidates may not meet every single role requirement listed above. If your experience looks a little different from our requirements but you believe that you can still bring value to the role, we’d love to see your application!

Who We Are:

We’re Criteo, the Commerce Intelligence Platform. Criteo helps businesses turn shopper signals into commerce outcomes while delivering more relevant experiences for shoppers. We use proprietary commerce intelligence and AI decisioning to drive relevance for shoppers and performance for businesses.

At Criteo, our culture is as unique as it is diverse. From our offices across the globe or from the comfort of home, our 3,600 Criteos collaborate together to build an open, impactful, and forward-thinking environment.

We foster a workplace where everyone is valued, and employment decisions are based solely on skills, qualifications, and business needs—never on non-job-related factors or legally protected characteristics.

What We Offer:

🏢 Ways of working – Our hybrid model blends home with in-office experiences, making space for both.

📈 Grow with us – Learning, mentorship & career development programs.

💪 Your wellbeing matters – Health benefits, wellness perks & mental health support.

🤝 A team that cares – Diverse, inclusive, and globally connected.

💸 Fair pay & perks – Attractive salary, with performance-based rewards and family-friendly policies, plus the potential for equity depending on role and level.

Additional benefits may vary depending on the country where you work and the nature of your employment with Criteo.

Title: Data Analyst / Engineer
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
¿Qué estamos buscando?




Actualmente nos encontramos en búsqueda de nuestro/nuestra futuro/futura Data Specialist cuya misión es evaluar, proponer y diseñar soluciones informacionales, así como su adecuada distribución, de manera ágil y alineada con las necesidades tácticas y estratégicas del negocio, garantizando el soporte necesario para el correcto direccionamiento de la Red Comercial.







Hablemos del Proyecto...




Como Data Specialist tus responsabilidades serán principalmente:

Generación, mantenimiento y planificación de informes del portal de información para dar respuesta a las peticiones de Red de forma ágil y en un Time to market óptimo.
Conocer los diferentes orígenes de datos corporativos.
Realizar desarrollos que integren información procedente de las diferentes unidades del Banco o bien de las BBDD corporativas, utilizando para ello las herramientas de desarrollo provistas por Banco Sabadell.
Tratamiento y análisis masivo de datos, prestando detalle en la calidad de estos.







¿Qué valoramos de tu candidatura?




Has estudiado Licenciatura / Grado en Ingeniería, Ciencia de Datos, Matemáticas o Estadística.
Tienes conocimiento de los lenguajes PL/SQL, además valoraremos muy positivamente si conoces la herramienta PowerBI.
Valoraremos el conocimiento de otras herramientas de gestión de datos (SAS, Microstrategy, etc).
Tienes experiencia de 1-2 años previa como Analista de Datos o tratamiento del dato.
Tienes preferencia por pertenecer al Colectivo Data o tienes interés de formar parte.
Cuentas con experiencia en análisis de datos y en el uso de herramientas que permiten su tratamiento de manera automatizada.
Tienes conocimiento del negocio a nivel comercial y de producto.







¿Qué te ofrecemos?




Serás parte de un equipo de profesionales de primer nivel, con una excelente reputación en el sector.
Podrás disfrutar de una amplia oferta formativa.
Aprenderás de la mano de Managers de referencia de las distintas direcciones.
Ampliarás tu red de contactos a través de la colaboración y espacios de networking.
Contarás con opciones de desarrollo de carrera y movilidad dentro del Grupo. Somos un grupo bancario que sienta sus bases en los principios de meritocracia y el desarrollo de sus colaboradores.
Contratación indefinida y remuneración competitiva.







Banco Sabadell está comprometido en promover ambientes de trabajo en los que se trate con respeto y dignidad a las personas, procurando el desarrollo profesional de la plantilla y garantizando la igualdad de oportunidades en su selección, formación y promoción ofreciendo un entorno de trabajo libre de cualquier discriminación por motivo de género, edad, orientación sexual, religión, etnia o cualquier otra circunstancia personal o social.




Banco Sabadell forma parte de la red de empresas con el distintivo de “Igualdad en la Empresa” otorgado por el Ministerio de Igualdad.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
About Us

With over 185 years of history, we are a leading health and wellness company. Our portfolio includes iconic brands such as Aquilea, Fisiocrem, Laborest, Sidroga, Biodramina, Aerored, Fave de Fuca, Halibut, Ems, and Depuralina, with a strong presence across major European markets.

We believe in the power of nature, enhanced by science, as the best way to improve human health and wellbeing sustainably. Our goal is to create a positive impact on both people's lives and the planet. We are currently seeking a highly motivated and skilled Junior Data Analyst to join our team.

Your Mission

We are currently looking to strengthen our Data team by appointing a Junior Data Analyst who will support the development of reporting and data visualization solutions across key areas of the business. As part of the Data Department, you will work closely with senior data profiles and business stakeholders, acting as a bridge between data and decision-making. You will contribute to transforming data into clear, reliable, and actionable insights, supporting a data-driven culture in an international and dynamic environment.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

In this role, you will be expected to:


Design, build, and maintain dashboards and reports in Power BI for different business areas (e.g., sales, operations, finance)
Gather and understand reporting requirements from business users, translating needs into clear and insightful visualizations
Perform exploratory data analysis to identify trends, anomalies, and improvement opportunities
Ensure data quality and consistency across reports by identifying and addressing discrepancies
Collaborate with Data Engineering teams to support the development of optimized data models for reporting
Support the automation of recurring reports and assist users in understanding and using dashboards effectively


Sector: Industria farmacéutica

Función: Ingeniería / Proyectos

Dirige a otros: No

Nivel profesional: Empleado nivel inicial

You'll excel if you have...


1-2 years of experience in data analysis, reporting, or similar roles (relevant internships are also considered).
Solid hands-on experience with Power BI (dashboard creation, data modeling basics, DAX, Power Query), along with strong SQL skills for data extraction and transformation, and advanced Excel capabilities
Good understanding of data modeling concepts (e.g., star schema, table relationships) and experience working with structured data sources
Strong analytical mindset with high attention to detail, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and reliability of data and reporting outputs
Ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, translating business needs into actionable insights
Proactive, organized, and eager to learn, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic environment
Familiarity with additional BI tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Qlik), cloud environments (Azure, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks), or business systems such as SAP, Salesforce, or Odoo will be considered a plus
Show more

Title: Data Analyst I Logistics Global HQ
Description:
Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

YOUR MISSION

Support the optimization of logistics features, with a focus on delivery areas and supply/demand imbalances, by maintaining and improving settings, quantifying their impact, providing data-driven insights, coordinating feature rollouts, and measuring their effectiveness.

THE JOURNEY


Work closely with local/regional stakeholders, product teams and development teams, serving as a bridge between product and operations, to define how to best set up and enable products, monitor and give visibility of metrics and KPIs.
Deep-dive on business metrics, in order to explore areas of opportunity and enable data-driven decision-making, based on applied statistics and probability techniques
Develop and maintain scalable and self-service visualization tools and storytelling dashboards.
Build optimization processes to define the best tuning of every feature on each geography.
Build robust relationships with stakeholders acting as a strategic and supportive link between the technology, business and product.
Support recurrent processes within the team, enabling the adjustment of tools to business needs
Continuously explore and refine key operational metrics (delivery time, efficiency, costs), gaining a comprehensive understanding of Glovo’s logistics.


Qualifications

WHAT YOU WILL BRING TO THE RIDE


1-2 years of professional experience outside of an academic and internship setting, in a quantitative analysis role
Excellent SQL and Excel/GoogleSheet skills
Solid proficiency in statistical programming (Python/R) and experience working with popular tools such as Pandas, SciPy, Seaborn, Jupyter/iPython notebooks, R Markdown.
Knowledge of visualization tools like Tableau, Looker, PowerBI, ...
Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills
Great spoken and written English.


Individuals representing diverse profiles, encompassing various genders, ethnicities, and backgrounds, are less likely to apply for this role if they do not possess solid experience in 100% of these areas. Even if it seems you don't meet our musts don't let it stop you, we are all about finding the best talent out there! Skills can be learned, and embracing diversity is invaluable.

We Believe Driven Talent Deserves


💪 Top-notch private health insurance to keep you at your peak.
🍔 Monthly Glovo credit to satisfy your cravings!
💳 Cobee discounts on transportation, food, and even kindergarten expenses.
🏊 Discounted gym memberships to keep you energized.
🏖️ Extra time off, the freedom to work from home two days a week, and the opportunity to work from anywhere for up to three weeks a year!
👪 Enhanced parental leave, and office-based nursery.
🧠 Online therapy and wellbeing benefits to ensure your mental well-being.


Here at Glovo, we thrive on diversity, we believe it enhances our teams, products, and culture. We know that the best ideas come from a mashup of brilliant diverse minds. This is why we are committed to providing equal opportunities to talent from all backgrounds - all genders, racial/diverse backgrounds, abilities, ages, sexual orientations and all other unique characteristics that make you YOU. We will encourage you to bring your authentic self to work, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels heard.

Feel free to note your pronouns in your application (e.g., she/her/hers, he/him/his, they/them/theirs, etc).

So, ready to take the wheel and make this the ride of your life?

Delve into our culture by taking a peek at our Instagram and check out our Linkedin and website!

Additional Information

At Glovo, your success is defined by both results and behaviors. We hire for excellence in craft and for the Leadership Principles that shape how we think, decide, and collaborate. What you achieve matters and how you achieve it defines us. Together, they move the business forward and deliver great experiences. Learn more about our Leadership Principles here.

Title: Project Analyst Trainee
Description:
Job Summary

Supports project manager by collecting data, preparing reports, and monitoring project schedules and deliverables. Also, supports in definition of requirements, system configuration, conducting testing and training activities.

Key Accountabilities

Data Analysis & Reporting: Assisting in collecting, cleaning, and visualizing data to generate insights for project performance, including creating reports and presentations in Excel/PowerPoint.
Project Coordination: Supporting daily project tasks, maintaining documentation, and updating project plans, timelines, and budgets.
Collaboration: Working with technical and business teams, aiding in stakeholder communication, traning sessions and preparing for User Acceptance Testing (UAT).
Functional Requirements Support: Assist in gathering and documenting business needs to translate them into clear functional requirements for the development or technical teams.
System Configuration: Support the setup and fine-tuning of system parameters, tools, or software modules according to project specifications and business rules.




Networking/Key relationships

Project Manager & Team Leads: regular reporting on project status, risks, and milestones to ensure alignment with the overall roadmap.
Users & Internal Clients: Collaborating to gather business needs, helping define functional requirements, and facilitating communication during the project lifecycle.
Technical & Development Teams: acting as a bridge to translate business requirements into technical tasks and assisting in system configuration troubleshooting.
External Vendors (when applicable): coordinating with third-party providers for software setup, updates, or technical support.

Minimum Knowledge required for the position:

Current Postgraduate or Higher Vocational Student
Undergraduate or Vocational Training Students, college courses in accounting, finance or systems engineering
Basic knowledge in accounting principles and administrative processes will a plus
Basic Microsoft Office user, specifically Excel
Languages: Spanish and English







Skills & Capabilities:

Team player
Flexibility and adaptability to fast changing environment
Results-oriented
Well organized and a self-starter
Detail-oriented, professional attitude, reliable

Title: Digital Analyst
Description:
Buscamos Digital Analytics Specialist en Barcelona 📊

En Shakers buscamos a alguien que sepa optimizar user flows, funnels y tracking cross-touchpoint (web + app) para trabajar con una empresa del sector de automoción premium.

El reto: convertir datos de comportamiento en insights que muevan la aguja del producto.




Lo importante 📌

💼 Freelance vía Shakers
📅 Duración: 8 meses aprox. con posibilidad de renovación
⏱️ Full time
📍 Híbrido en Barcelona, mínimo 1 día/semana presencial




Qué harás 🎯

Analizar comportamiento, funnels y puntos de drop-off, y traducirlo en recomendaciones accionables
Construir y mantener dashboards en Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics y Looker Studio
Diseñar y orquestar tracking concepts en web y app, garantizando calidad del dato
Apoyar iniciativas de personalización y segmentación de audiencias
Trabajar codo a codo con product, UX research y leadership




Qué necesitamos sí o sí ✅

Dominio de Google Analytics + Google Tag Manager + Adobe Analytics
Experiencia diseñando tracking concepts, taxonomías y modelos de eventos
Manejo de Looker Studio o Tableau




Suma puntos ⭐

Experiencia en automoción o premium consumer brands
Plataformas de A/B testing
SQL / BigQuery

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
El Data Analyst será responsable de recopilar, estructurar y analizar datos para generar insights que apoyen la toma de decisiones estratégicas en activaciones, contenido, talento, partnerships y productos basados en IA.


Trabajará directamente con el Head of Business Intelligence para construir dashboards, reports, modelos de seguimiento de campañas y análisis de audiencias y fandoms. Su misión es convertir datos complejos en información útil, clara y accionable.





Requisitos



Responsabilidades Principales:
1. Análisis de Datos e Insights


Recopilar y analizar datos de diferentes fuentes: social media, CRM, plataformas de activación, herramientas de listening y performance.


Identificar patrones, comportamientos, fandoms y oportunidades de segmentación para marcas, talentos y properties.


Generar reports semanales/mensuales con conclusiones claras y recomendaciones.


2. Dashboards & Reporting


• Construir dashboards en Power BI, Tableau o Looker para medir: o Rendimiento de campañas
o Engagement
o Conversión fan → cliente

o Crecimiento de audiencias


o Performance de contenido y talento
• Automatizar reporting para que equipos creativos, estrategia y cliente tengan

datos en tiempo real.


3. Social Listening & Trend Analysis


Operar herramientas como Brandwatch, Talkwalker, Meltwater o Pulsar.


Mapear conversaciones, microcomunidades, tendencias culturales y temas


emergentes.


Identificar insights clave para briefing, creatividad y storytelling.


4. Medición de ROI & Performance


• Apoyar en la creación de modelos de atribución de activaciones: o Leads generados


o Incremento de ventas estimado o Lift de marca
o Costes y eficiencia

• Medir el impacto de activaciones con talento, avatares y contenido IA. 5. Soporte a Proyectos y Propuestas


Preparar análisis que alimenten pitches y presentaciones estratégicas.


Transformar datos en gráficas claras, storytelling y conclusiones accionables.


Participar en el desarrollo de productos propios (AI Insights Engine,


dashboards, etc.).


Perfil Requerido: Experiencia


1–3 años en roles de Data Analyst, Digital Analyst, Research Analyst o similar.


Valorable experiencia en marketing, deportes, entretenimiento o redes sociales.


Experiencia con datasets grandes o desestructurados. Habilidades Técnicas


Excel avanzado + SQL (nivel intermedio).


Familiaridad con Python o R (no imprescindible pero deseable).


Manejo de herramientas BI (Power BI, Tableau, Looker).


Uso de plataformas de listening (Brandwatch, Talkwalker, Meltwater...).


Entendimiento de métricas de social media y performance.


Habilidades Blandas


Capacidad de sintetizar y explicar insights de forma visual y clara.


Curiosidad intelectual y mentalidad de investigación.


Detallista, ordenado y orientado a resultados.


Buena comunicación y capacidad de trabajar en equipo.


KPIs Clave del Rol


Precisión y claridad de los análisis entregados.


Calidad y escalabilidad de dashboards desarrollados.


Cumplimiento de timings y eficiencia en el reporting.


Contribución a pitches y propuestas ganadas.


Adopción interna de insights por parte de equipos creativos y de estrategia.

Title: DATA ANALYST
Description:
About

Joko helps consumers shop smarter. Their ambition is to revolutionize how people shop, empowering them to find what they need, make informed decisions, and save money.

Joko is a tech company founded in Paris. Their team of 90+ talents is international and spread across Paris, Barcelona, and New York, with some working remotely.

As a certified B Corporation, they are committed to making a difference for the communities they serve, including the 6 million consumers already using Joko to save money every day at over 10,000 merchants.

Over the years, they have expanded their value proposition to help consumers make smarter shopping decisions with features such as cash back, buy now pay later, automatic coupons, price drop alerts, carbon footprint tracking, and more. Today, they are dedicating significant resources to an AI-powered assistant that helps users find the right product based on criteria like price, quality, or environmental impact across the global e-commerce catalog.

After reaching profitability in their core market, they are expanding internationally with a primary focus on the US.

It’s still day 1 at Joko; come build the future of shopping with Joko.

Job Description

At Joko, we help consumers shop smarter. Our mission is to revolutionize shopping, empowering people to find what they need, make informed decisions, and save money.

Founded in Paris, Joko is a tech company and certified B Corp with over 90 talents across Paris, Barcelona, and New York (and beyond). More than 6 million users already save money every day at 10,000+ merchants with Joko.

From cashback and automatic coupons to price alerts and carbon tracking, we keep expanding our products to make shopping smarter. We’re now building an AI-powered shopping assistant to help users find the best products by price, quality, and impact.

Having reached profitability in our core market, we’re now scaling globally, with a strong focus on the US.

It’s still day 1, come build the future of shopping with us!

This position is remote-friendly.

🧚🏻 Our Data team

The Data team at Joko is turning mountains of data into actionable insights for all the teams! The Data team is part of our Operations department led by our COO, and our mission is to empower the company to make informed decisions.

More Specifically, The Team's Responsibilities Are


State-of-the-art analytics & data science: We want to be at the forefront of analytics and data science by building top-notch analyses and tools. Our goal is to allow teams to leverage powerful insights from advanced reporting and predictive models.
AI-driven autonomy for stakeholders: We are doubling down on the combination of AI and data to unlock a new level of autonomy for our stakeholders, seamlessly bridging data insights with the AI tools we use, built on secure and trustworthy foundations.
Spread the data culture: At Joko, we have a strong engineering and tech culture across all teams. We work hand-in-hand with our stakeholders, providing support and training on data tools, and building close relationships to ensure our solutions align perfectly with their operational needs and evolve with them over time.


🎯 What you will do

As a Data Analyst, you will have a cross-functional role and collaborate closely with teams to understand our data and develop insights.


Advanced analytics & data science: You will explore and analyze data to uncover trends, patterns, and potential issues that will support decision-making processes across all teams. You will also contribute to data science initiatives by building predictive models and applying statistics to generate deeper insights and drive measurable business impact.
Reporting and Data Visualization: You will design and create dashboards and data visualizations that empower stakeholders to explore data autonomously. Leveraging AI tools to move faster, your focus will be on what matters most: defining the right metrics, surfacing meaningful insights, and ensuring data is understood and acted upon across the company.
AI and data integration: You will actively contribute to bridging our data with the AI tools we use, ensuring insights are integrated and maintained over time.
Stakeholder collaboration: You will work hand-in-hand with stakeholders to understand their needs, collect their feedback, and evangelize data usage to help them make smarter choices and become more autonomous over time.
Continuous Learning and Improvement: As "never stop learning" is one of our values, you will grow along the way and keep up with the latest analytical methods, AI developments, and tools in our data stack.


👀 Who we're looking for


Experience: You have 2+ years of professional experience in data analysis (relevant internships can count). We hire across different seniority levels, so we encourage you to apply even if you are more experienced.
Interest in AI: You are genuinely curious about AI and its applications, and eager to leverage it in your day-to-day work to move faster and create more impact.
Mindset: You have an entrepreneurial mindset, you like challenges, you welcome feedback and you are willing to get better every day to reach excellence.
Problem-solving & Analytical skills: You love solving complex problems and analyzing data.
Coding knowledge: Mastering SQL and Python is not mandatory, as you can still learn when joining, but it's a plus.
Business acumen: You can understand the needs of your business stakeholders and get the big picture of how the business is running.
Communication: You know how to present your conclusions clearly and concisely (written and oral).
Education: You have graduated from a top-tier business or engineering school.
Languages: You are fluent in English, both written and spoken.


💎 Our perks

(Some of the benefits listed below are available to full-time positions only)

At Joko, we believe that flexibility and trust are essential. Our work environment reflects this through:


Flexible remote : If you live in Paris, you can choose to work from our office or from home with no constraints. If you live elsewhere, we can provide access to a coworking space and a coworking budget.
Work from anywhere : Want to spend a month in Italy while working? You can work from most countries in the world for up to 3 months per year.


On Top Of That, We Offer Plenty Of Perks


💸 Top-market compensation
📈 Equity for everyone with the chance to own a piece of what you build
🤖 Half-day each week dedicated to leveling up with AI by exploring new tools, iterating hard, and sharpening your skills
🌴 Yearly offsite in amazing locations and budget for team-building events & monthly in-person gatherings
💪 Contribution to your ClassPass subscription
🍼 8-week leave paid 100% for the second parent
…and much more, check the full list here!


🤝 Our hiring process


Intro call: Quick screening with the Hiring Manager or the Talent team.
Step 1 – Team interview (45 min): Conversation with two Joko team members (could include the Hiring Manager, people from the team you’d join, or colleagues from other teams).
Step 2 – Role-specific assessments
For non-tech roles: Take-home case study followed by a 45 min interview. We assess both your output and how you think in real time. The exercise will be relevant to the role (e.g. analysis, strategy, or process design).
For tech roles: Live technical interviews:
Coding interview + System design interview
For research internships, an additional round may involve analyzing and presenting a research paper
Step 3 – Leadership interview (45 min): Conversation with a SteerCo member and a Founder.
References: Up to 3 calls with former colleagues or managers.

☕ You may also be invited for coffee with team members to get a feel for our culture.

Additional Information


Contract Type: Full-Time
Location: Paris, Barcelona
Possible full remote

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Tareas



Análisis del uso que hace el cliente de los datos y estratégia de negocio.


Detección de oportunidades de explotación de sus datos y de datos externos con alto impacto en su negocio.


Exploración y análisis de datos, identificación de variables relevantes para resolver retos concretos.


Estudio y modelización de la estructura global de datos del cliente.


Construcción de soluciones a problemas de negocio en base a datos de principio a fin (compresión de necesidad, integrción del dato, curación de datos, visualización con herramientas de business intelligence, construcción de algoritmos y modelos predictivos).


Comunicación de resultados e interacción directa con clientes.



Requisitos



Titulación: Grado en Matemáticas, Física, Ingeniería, Química. Máster en BI, Business Analytics, Data Science, o experiencia equivalente.


Herramientas: SQL, Python


Nivel de Español hablado y escrito nativo.


Sensibilidad de negocio y pensamiento estratégico.


Fuertes habilidades analíticas


Capacidad de comunicación y presentación de resultados analíticos



Beneficios



Participar en proyectos innovadores en el ámbito del Data Science (nuestra tecnología combina BI con soluciones de machine learning adaptadas a cada cliente y con las que él mismo interacciona).


Contacto directo con los clientes y acceso a problemas reales a los que se enfrentan las empresas, diseñando soluciones con un alto impacto.


Formar parte de una startup en crecimiento y colaborar a diario con un equipo de expertos en consultoría estrategia, data science y desarrollo de software en la nube.


Trabajar con tecnologías de última generación





Beneficios Prenomics





Horario de trabajo flexible e híbrido


Suministro ilimitado de bebidas con y sin cafeína


Horas semanales dedicadas a apreder y discutir nuevas tecnologías


Sistema de retribución flexible: tickets restaurante y transporte


Evaluaciones de desempeño, diseño de plan de carrera y revisiones salariales anuales


Actividades de teambuilding con todo el equipo

Title: BUSINESS & DATA ANALYST
Description:
Grupo HD Covalco es una empresa líder en el sector de distribución y alimentación, con una red consolidada de supermercados y franquicias en toda España. Nuestro objetivo es ofrecer productos de calidad y un servicio excepcional, adaptándonos a las necesidades de nuestros clientes y comunidades.

Estamos buscando un/a BUSINESS & DATA ANALYST para la central de Granollers.




Responsabilidades

Análisis de negocio y reporting

- Elaboración de informes sobre ventas, clientes y centros.

- Desarrollo de dashboards en Power BI.

- Reportes a Dirección.

Gestión avanzada de datos

- Uso avanzado de Excel (tablas dinámicas, Power Query, fórmulas).

- Uso de IA para automatización y análisis.

- Mejora de estructuras de datos.

Optimización de procesos

- Automatización de tareas repetitivas.

- Reducción de tiempos de análisis.

- Estandarización de informes.

Ejecución operativa de compras

- Gestión de repartos definidos.

- Seguimiento de incidencias.

- Coordinación con Compras.

Soporte comercial y Call Center

- Atención a centros y clientes.

- Resolución de dudas comerciales.

- Gestión y seguimiento de incidencias.




Requisitos

-Formación: Grado en ADE o Economía

-Experiencia previa en distribución

-Conocimiento del mercado

-Dominio Excel

-Residir por la zona




Habilidades

-Capacidad analítica

-Organización y planificación

-Comunicación

-Proactividad

-Adaptabilidad




Qué ofrecemos?

-Contrato indefinido

-Jornada completa

-Paquete retributivo competitivo

-Incorporación inmediata

-Formar parte de una gran compañía en expansión




Si estás interesado/a en la oferta laboral contacta con nosotros. ¡Queremos conocerte!




En Grupo HD Covalco, promovemos un entorno de trabajo inclusivo y respetuoso, en el que cada persona puede desarrollar su potencial al máximo. Promovemos la diversidad e inclusión en todas las formas, apoyando y garantizando la igualdad de oportunidades y la no discriminación por razón de género, orientación sexual, edad, raza o religión. Creemos firmemente en aprovechar las capacidades de las personas en toda su diversidad, evitando cualquier tipo de discriminación.

Buscamos a alguien entusiasta y con ganas de ayudarnos a crecer. Si crees que encajas con esta descripción, ¡nos encantaría saber de ti!

Grupo HD Covalco es una empresa líder en el sector de distribución y alimentación, con una red consolidada de supermercados y franquicias en toda España. Nuestro objetivo es ofrecer productos de calidad y un servicio excepcional, adaptándonos a las necesidades de nuestros clientes y comunidades.

Estamos buscando un/a BUSINESS & DATA ANALYST para la central de Granollers.


Responsabilidades

Análisis de negocio y reporting

- Elaboración de informes sobre ventas, clientes y centros.

- Desarrollo de dashboards en Power BI.

- Reportes a Dirección.

Gestión avanzada de datos

- Uso avanzado de Excel (tablas dinámicas, Power Query, fórmulas).

- Uso de IA para automatización y análisis.

- Mejora de estructuras de datos.

Optimización de procesos

- Automatización de tareas repetitivas.

- Reducción de tiempos de análisis.

- Estandarización de informes.

Ejecución operativa de compras

- Gestión de repartos definidos.

- Seguimiento de incidencias.

- Coordinación con Compras.

Soporte comercial y Call Center

- Atención a centros y clientes.

- Resolución de dudas comerciales.

- Gestión y seguimiento de incidencias.


Requisitos

-Formación: Grado en ADE o Economía

-Experiencia previa en distribución

-Conocimiento del mercado

-Dominio Excel

-Residir por la zona


Habilidades

-Capacidad analítica

-Organización y planificación

-Comunicación

-Proactividad

-Adaptabilidad


Qué ofrecemos?

-Contrato indefinido

-Jornada completa

-Paquete retributivo competitivo

-Incorporación inmediata

-Formar parte de una gran compañía en expansión


Si estás interesado/a en la oferta laboral contacta con nosotros. ¡Queremos conocerte!


En Grupo HD Covalco, promovemos un entorno de trabajo inclusivo y respetuoso, en el que cada persona puede desarrollar su potencial al máximo. Promovemos la diversidad e inclusión en todas las formas, apoyando y garantizando la igualdad de oportunidades y la no discriminación por razón de género, orientación sexual, edad, raza o religión. Creemos firmemente en aprovechar las capacidades de las personas en toda su diversidad, evitando cualquier tipo de discriminación.

Buscamos a alguien entusiasta y con ganas de ayudarnos a crecer. Si crees que encajas con esta descripción, ¡nos encantaría saber de ti!

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Who we are:
At Rover, pets and their people are at the heart of everything we do. We connect pet parents with trusted pet care across the U.S., Canada, Europe, and Australia.

Headquartered in Seattle and Barcelona, we’re a values driven, fast-growing tech company focused on building safe and personalized experiences tailored to the needs of each unique pet. We’re investing in AI as a business accelerator and provide every team member access to AI tools in service of creating better experiences for our community.

We are proud to be recognized as a great place to work, having been named among the 100 Best Companies to Work For in Seattle Business Magazine and Washington’s Best Workplaces in the Puget Sound Business Journal. At Rover we’re committed to creating an accessible, inclusive, and welcoming community, which starts with our employees.

Want to make an impact? Join our pack and come work (and play!) with us.

Who we're looking for

We're looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our Barcelona Marketing Analytics team, partnering closely with Lifecycle Marketing (our CRM / retention function) to shape how Rover measures and grows long-term customer value.

The Barcelona team's north star is simple: drive more repeat bookings and lengthen LTV across both pet parents and sitters. Everything we do, from email and push experimentation to attribution work to measurement framework design, ladders up to that.

You'll own meaningful pieces of that work as an independent contributor. You'll be the person who turns messy marketing data into reporting that leadership can actually lean on; who partners with our Lifecycle Marketing, Martech, and Analytics Engineering teams to QA business logic and keep our data clean; and who designs and analyzes tests, not only classic A/B tests, but cross-channel retargeting and BD experiments where the measurement gets harder. When our holdout strategy hits its limits, you'll be part of the team figuring out what comes next.

This role rewards intellectual curiosity. Our incrementality analyses wrestle with real puzzles: mix-shift between markets, novelty-effect decay on mature cohorts, holdout leakage, and interaction between transactional and marketing sends. We don't expect you to have solved all of these before—we expect you to be genuinely excited to dig in.

And because Rover is a two-sided marketplace, you'll hold both sides in mind. The best analyses we do connect the dots.

Your impact
Own reporting that Marketing actually trusts. Build and maintain the dashboards, data models, and reporting layers that Lifecycle Marketing and broader Marketing leadership rely on day-to-day. Proactively identify where our reporting is noisy, inconsistent, or missing, and fix it. You'll be the go-to person Marketing leads come to when a number looks off.

Design and analyze experiments. Own the end-to-end lifecycle of tests in your area—from scoping the hypothesis and picking the right measurement, to running the analysis, to writing up the result and socializing what we learned. You'll partner with Lifecycle Marketing on their monthly testing cadence, and you'll take on harder cross-channel and retargeting tests where the measurement isn't obvious.

Support the evolution of our measurement framework. You'll partner with the team on incrementality work, stratified holdouts, and attribution model refinement—bringing statistical rigor to questions like how much lift is real, how much is novelty, and how much is noise?

Be the analytical bridge to Martech. Partner with our Martech and Analytics Engineering teams to QA business logic in our CRM pipelines, validate audience data, and make sure the campaigns Lifecycle Marketing launches are measurable from day one. You'll be the analytics voice in conversations about tooling and data flow.

Unblock the team. A senior IC on this team makes everyone around them more effective. You'll review SQL, pressure-test analytical approaches, and help raise the bar on how we structure our work.

Tell stories that drive action. Root-cause analysis is core to this role: when a metric moves, you'll go find out why, tie it to marketplace dynamics on both the demand and supply side, and bring a clear narrative to the people who need to act on it.

Your Qualifications


3+ years of experience using data to drive business impact, including identifying actionable insights, crafting compelling narratives, and partnering with cross-functional teams to define and implement solutions.
Strong proficiency in SQL—confident writing complex queries against large event-based datasets, and comfortable reviewing and improving code from other analysts.
Hands-on experience building and maintaining transformation models (dbt or similar) and a point of view on what makes a good data model.
Solid grounding in statistical analysis using Python or R—hypothesis testing, regression, bootstrapping—and an understanding of the trade-offs between different measurement approaches.
Proven experience owning A/B testing end-to-end: experimental design, sample size, analysis, and write-up.
A clear communicator who can make complex analyses land with both technical and non-technical audiences, and who enjoys partnering directly with business stakeholders.
Demonstrated ability to manage analytical work independently—scoping, prioritizing, and delivering without needing to be micromanaged.
Data visualization tool experience (e.g., Mode, Looker, Tableau).
Genuine intellectual curiosity about measurement problems, and comfort sitting with ambiguity while you figure them out.




Nice to have


Experience supporting a Lifecycle Marketing, CRM, or retention function, particularly with email, push, or SMS programs.
Familiarity with marketing attribution concepts (MMM, last-touch, multi-touch) and an interest in where attribution modeling is headed.
Exposure to incrementality testing, causal impact models, geo-based experiments, or similar approaches beyond classic A/B holdouts.
Experience in a two-sided marketplace, with a gut feel for how supply and demand dynamics show up in the data.
Familiarity with any part of our stack: Amazon Redshift, dbt, Mode, Statsig, Amplitude, Iterable, or Hightouch.
Track record of mentoring junior analysts or raising the technical bar on a team without formal management responsibility.




Benefits Of Working At Rover


Long-term incentive plan with a company performance-based cash payout
Pension plan
Private medical insurance
25 days PTO
Meal allowance and flexible compensation plan (transport and nursery)
Gym membership
€450 to cover the costs associated with the adoption of a pet
Annual €150 wellness reimbursement
Flexible work hours, sometimes you'll need to be in at certain times, but on the whole, we're pretty flexible when it comes to managing workload and time
Grab snacks, fresh fruit, in our kitchen to keep yourself going
Regular team activities, events, game nights, and more
Dog-friendly office




Rover is an equal-opportunity employer committed to promoting a diverse, inclusive, and inventive environment with the best employees. We’re driven by seeing our people succeed and grow, and we work to ensure everyone contributes to their fullest potential. We consider all qualified applicants without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, protected veteran status, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances.

Title: Data Analyst, Performance Media EMEA
Description:
What You'll Do:

As a Data Analyst in our Analytics, Performance Media, Travel team, you will drive advertising strategies thanks to your oversight of business objectives and technical expertise of our products. You will answer the analytical needs of a set of our largest performance media clients in the travel industry.


Client insights: you will analyse complex data from multiple sources to understand the performance of our advertisers’ campaigns and generate innovative and impactful insights
Business strategy: you will partner with account executives, account strategists, and campaign managers to help drive client strategy and direction
Client interactions: you will present some of your insights directly to clients in your portfolio, often through videoconference and occasionally in person for which there may be a need to travel
Reporting tools and metrics: you will develop and scale reports and metrics that will present actionable insights to teams across the business and strategic leadership.
Testing: you will design, monitor and analyse A/B tests to understand better how our products work for clients.
Collaborative projects: you will work on ad hoc projects with different teams to automate analyses globally, improve our internal processes and our way of working and scale knowledge across the company



Who You Are:


At least 1 year of experience in data analysis.
A problem-solver with a passion for analytics and a logical approach to challenges.
Able to solve data problems using Python, SQL, and enthusiastic about automating and scaling solutions.
Experience using Generative AI solutions to augment data exploration, automate insights, or generate analytical narratives is strongly preferred.
Business-minded with a goal to turn data insights into actionable strategies.
Team player who thrives in a collaborative, inclusive, and vibrant workspace.
You are skilled at simplifying complex concepts for diverse audiences, both virtually and in person.
Experience in online advertising, marketing, or consulting will be a plus.
Fluent in English.
Must be eligible to work in Spain full-time with no restrictions.



We acknowledge that many candidates may not meet every single role requirement listed above. If your experience looks a little different from our requirements but you believe that you can still bring value to the role, we’d love to see your application!

Who We Are:

We’re Criteo, the Commerce Intelligence Platform. Criteo helps businesses turn shopper signals into commerce outcomes while delivering more relevant experiences for shoppers. We use proprietary commerce intelligence and AI decisioning to drive relevance for shoppers and performance for businesses.

At Criteo, our culture is as unique as it is diverse. From our offices across the globe or from the comfort of home, our 3,600 Criteos collaborate together to build an open, impactful, and forward-thinking environment.

We foster a workplace where everyone is valued, and employment decisions are based solely on skills, qualifications, and business needs—never on non-job-related factors or legally protected characteristics.

What We Offer:

🏢 Ways of working – Our hybrid model blends home with in-office experiences, making space for both.

📈 Grow with us – Learning, mentorship & career development programs.

💪 Your wellbeing matters – Health benefits, wellness perks & mental health support.

🤝 A team that cares – Diverse, inclusive, and globally connected.

💸 Fair pay & perks – Attractive salary, with performance-based rewards and family-friendly policies, plus the potential for equity depending on role and level.

Additional benefits may vary depending on the country where you work and the nature of your employment with Criteo.
