Title: Analista de datos
Description:
Descripción de la oferta En nuestro Programa Talento Joven buscamos personas recién tituladas en Formación Profesional o recién graduadas en universidad en informática, análisis de datos o programación, con interés en adquirir experiencia y conocimientos prácticos en el campo del Data Science y el análisis de datos.




Funciones:

Participar en un programa formativo de 30 semanas enfocado en habilidades técnicas y prácticas de análisis de datos
Trabajar sobre proyectos reales y simulaciones del entorno empresarial, incluyendo retos de predicción, análisis exploratorio y visualización de datos
Colaborar y aprender directamente de profesionales en activo que te acompañarán durante tu evolución
Practicar con herramientas como Python, SQL, Pandas, Power BI, Jupyter Notebooks y Scikit-learn
Aplicar procedimientos y buenas prácticas de análisis en entornos controlados de laboratorio







Lo que ofrecemos:

Plan formativo diseñado y actualizado según las necesidades reales del mercado laboral en data
Formación práctica, en grupo reducido, orientada al trabajo que se realiza en equipos de data science
Mentoring personalizado con especialistas del sector
Posibilidad real de entrevista para ofertas de empleo al finalizar el programa en empresas colaboradoras
Red de contacto con profesionales y exalumnos del programa







Requisitos:

Ciclo Formativo de Grado Medio o Superior relacionado con informática, análisis de datos o programación, o Grado universitario en Ingeniería Informática, Matemáticas, Estadística o disciplinas relacionadas con datos
Interés por el análisis de datos y motivación para afrontar retos prácticos
Capacidad para trabajar en equipo, comunicarte y aprender en un entorno técnico







Tipo de puesto: Formativo/Prácticas Ubicación: Modalidad híbrida (presencial y online)

Duración: 30 semanas Incorporación: Octubre 2026

Title: Freelance Data Science Engineer (Python & SQL)
Description:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:


Design original computational data science problems that simulate real-world analytical workflows across industries (telecom, finance, government, e-commerce, healthcare)
Create problems requiring Python programming to solve (using Pandas, Numpy, Scipy, Sklearn, Statsmodels, Matplotlib, Seaborn)
Ensure problems are computationally intensive and cannot be solved manually within reasonable timeframes (days/weeks)
Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains in data processing, statistical analysis, feature engineering, predictive modeling, and insight extraction
Create deterministic problems with reproducible answers: avoid stochastic elements or require fixed random seeds for exact reproducibility
Base problems on real business challenges: customer analytics, risk assessment, fraud detection, forecasting, optimization, and operational efficiency
Design end-to-end problems spanning the complete data science pipeline (data ingestion → cleaning → EDA → modeling → validation → deployment considerations)
Incorporate big data processing scenarios requiring scalable computational approaches
Verify solutions using Python with standard data science libraries and statistical methods
Document problem statements clearly with realistic business contexts and provide verified correct answers


What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for Data Science specialists with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:


5+ years of hands-on data science experience with proven business impact
Portfolio of completed projects and publications showcasing real-world problem-solving
Expert Python programming for data science (pandas, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, statsmodels)
Expert statistical analysis and machine learning - deep understanding of algorithms, methods, and their practical applications
Expert with SQL and database operations for data manipulation and analysis
Experience with GenAI technologies (LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, vector databases)
Understanding of MLOps practices and model deployment workflows
Knowledge of modern frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain)
Strong written English (C1+)


How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $34 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Data Analyst in Spain.

This is a highly cross-functional data role sitting at the core of a modern Customer Data Platform initiative, where your work directly influences acquisition, conversion, segmentation, and retention strategies across multiple brands. You will operate between business stakeholders and engineering teams, translating product and business needs into clear, executable data specifications while ensuring the integrity and usability of a rapidly evolving data platform. The environment is technical, fast-moving, and foundational, with a strong focus on building reliable data structures rather than traditional reporting. You will work on identity resolution, semantic modeling, and data quality systems that power downstream analytics and marketing activation. A significant part of your role is analytical exploration, validating hypotheses and shaping data models before they are productionized. This is a hands-on, high-impact position where your work defines how trusted and usable data becomes across the entire organization.

Accountabilities


Translate business requirements into detailed technical data specifications, including source schemas, transformations, data models, quality checks, and consumption logic.
Work closely with engineering teams to ensure data solutions are implemented correctly and aligned with business intent, staying involved throughout the development lifecycle.
Perform hands-on data analysis (SQL, Python, dbt) to validate hypotheses, explore datasets, and support the design of scalable data models.
Own data quality issues end-to-end, identifying root causes, coordinating fixes with engineering teams, and validating downstream impact.
Contribute to the development of a unified semantic layer, ensuring consistent definitions, documentation, and usability of core business metrics.
Support and extend identity resolution systems within the Customer Data Platform, validating matching logic and data consistency across multiple sources.
Ensure delivered data products match agreed specifications and raise issues proactively before release to production.
Enable downstream analytics and business teams by ensuring data is reliable, well-structured, and clearly documented.


Requirements


Strong expertise in SQL, including complex queries, window functions, joins, and large-scale dataset troubleshooting.
Practical experience with dbt, including model development, testing, and debugging.
Solid Python skills for data exploration, scripting, and ad hoc analysis.
Experience working with modern data stacks and medallion/layered architectures (bronze, silver, gold).
Proven ability to write clear, actionable technical specifications that engineering teams can implement.
Hands-on experience owning data quality issues from detection to root cause resolution and validation.
Ability to communicate effectively with both technical engineers and non-technical business stakeholders.
Experience in iGaming, affiliate marketing, SaaS, or other high-volume digital environments is a plus.
Experience with customer identity resolution, Customer 360, or single customer view initiatives is highly valuable.


Benefits


Competitive salary with performance-based incentives.
Fully remote work across Europe and the UK.
Flexible working environment supporting autonomy and work-life balance.
Opportunity to contribute to a high-impact Customer Data Platform shaping business growth across multiple brands.
Exposure to modern data stack technologies and scalable data platform architecture.
Collaborative, fast-paced engineering culture focused on ownership and continuous improvement.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Database Administrator - Graduates - AI Training - Barcelona, Spain
Description:
Database Administrator - Graduates - AI Training

About Prolific

Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world.

Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills.

The role

We're looking for Database Administrator to join our Expert Network to help train and evaluate cutting-edge AI models using real data expertise. If you have the necessary experience, we'll send you a quick 10- to 15-minute test to assess your skills and suitability for AI tasks. If successful, you'll be invited to join Prolific as a participant, where you'll get paid to train and evaluate powerful AI models.

Researchers looking for your skills tend to pay up to $25 per hour. You must be prepared to complete paid tasks that require one hour of uninterrupted work, though many are shorter.

What you'll bring


Professional Experience: years of experience in high-volume data entry, data processing, database management, or records administration.
Accuracy & Attention to Detail: a proven track record of maintaining high accuracy rates across large datasets, with a sharp eye for inconsistencies, duplicates, and formatting errors.
Speed & Efficiency: high typing speed and the ability to process structured and unstructured data quickly without sacrificing quality.
Data Literacy: familiarity with data formats, validation rules, and the ability to identify when AI-generated outputs contain logical or factual errors.
Communication Skills: solid written English skills sufficient to assess clarity and correctness in AI-generated text.
Language Proficiency: multilingual capabilities are a significant plus, especially for evaluating data quality across localized datasets.
A PayPal account to receive payment from our clients


What you'll be doing in the role


Evaluate AI Data Outputs: review AI-generated data entries, extractions, and structured records for accuracy, completeness, and formatting consistency.
Simulate Data Entry Tasks: create realistic data entry scenarios and edge cases to test how AI handles messy inputs, ambiguous fields, or conflicting records.
Audit AI-Generated Datasets: review AI-produced data for errors in categorisation, labelling, or field mapping, and flag issues against standard data quality rubrics.
Annotation & Labelling: tag and classify data samples to help AI models learn correct data structures, formats, and validation rules.
Quality Assurance: compare AI outputs against established data entry standards to ensure they meet professional accuracy and consistency benchmarks.


Key Technologies


Data Tools: proficiency with Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, or database platforms such as Airtable, SQL, or Access.

About Prolific

Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world.

Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills.

The role

We're looking for Database Administrator to join our Expert Network to help train and evaluate cutting-edge AI models using real data expertise. If you have the necessary experience, we'll send you a quick 10- to 15-minute test to assess your skills and suitability for AI tasks. If successful, you'll be invited to join Prolific as a participant, where you'll get paid to train and evaluate powerful AI models.

Researchers looking for your skills tend to pay up to $25 per hour. You must be prepared to complete paid tasks that require one hour of uninterrupted work, though many are shorter.

What you'll bring

  • Professional Experience: years of experience in high-volume data entry, data processing, database management, or records administration.
  • Accuracy & Attention to Detail: a proven track record of maintaining high accuracy rates across large datasets, with a sharp eye for inconsistencies, duplicates, and formatting errors.
  • Speed & Efficiency: high typing speed and the ability to process structured and unstructured data quickly without sacrificing quality.
  • Data Literacy: familiarity with data formats, validation rules, and the ability to identify when AI-generated outputs contain logical or factual errors.
  • Communication Skills: solid written English skills sufficient to assess clarity and correctness in AI-generated text.
  • Language Proficiency: multilingual capabilities are a significant plus, especially for evaluating data quality across localized datasets.
  • A PayPal account to receive payment from our clients

What you'll be doing in the role

  • Evaluate AI Data Outputs: review AI-generated data entries, extractions, and structured records for accuracy, completeness, and formatting consistency.
  • Simulate Data Entry Tasks: create realistic data entry scenarios and edge cases to test how AI handles messy inputs, ambiguous fields, or conflicting records.
  • Audit AI-Generated Datasets: review AI-produced data for errors in categorisation, labelling, or field mapping, and flag issues against standard data quality rubrics.
  • Annotation & Labelling: tag and classify data samples to help AI models learn correct data structures, formats, and validation rules.
  • Quality Assurance: compare AI outputs against established data entry standards to ensure they meet professional accuracy and consistency benchmarks.

Key Technologies

  • Data Tools: proficiency with Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, or database platforms such as Airtable, SQL, or Access.

Title: Gestor/A De Recerca Per Donar Suport A Les Activitats De Direcció De L'Apec
Company: Institut d'Estudis Espacials de Catalunya - IEEC
Description:
IntroduccióL'objecte d'aquesta convocatòria és el procés de selecció d'un candidat per ocupar un lloc de treball de Gestor/a de Recerca adscrit a l'Àrea de Promoció del Sector Espacial de Catalunya de l'Institut d'Estudis Espacials de Catalunya.

L'IEEC és un institut de recerca que estudia totes les àrees de coneixement i tecnologies aplicades al sector espacial i a les ciències espacials, inclosa l'astrofísica, cosmologia, ciència planetària, observació de la Terra, navegació i l'enginyeria espacial.

La seva missió és impulsar les fronteres de la investigació espacial des dels àmbits científic i tecnològic per al màxim benefici de la societat.

Així mateix, l'IEEC també desenvolupa funcions de promoció del sector espacial de Catalunya i actua com l'entitat que impulsa i representa el sector a nivell nacional i internacional, activitat que es desenvolupa en el marc de l'Estratègia Catalunya Espai ****, impulsada per la Generalitat de Catalunya.L'IEEC (Institut d'Estudis Espacials de Catalunya) es va establir el febrer de **** com una fundació privada sense ànim de lucre (NPO) per fomentar la R+D a l'espai a Catalunya.

Actualment, la Fundació es troba adscrita a la Generalitat de Catalunya i forma part del seu sector públic institucional pel fet de tenir la Generalitat participació majoritària en l'entitat.L'Estratègia Catalunya Espai **** reforça l'ecosistema espacial mitjançant el llançament de missions de satèl·lits, el desenvolupament d'infraestructures terrestres, programes de recerca i innovació i de promoció de talent, programes d'impuls industrial i serveis d'alt valor afegit basats en tecnologies espacials.

L'IEEC participa en el desplegament de l'Estratègia, en col·laboració amb la Fundació i2cat i l'Institut Cartogràfic i Geològic de Catalunya (ICGC).

Les funcions de promoció del sector espacial que du a terme l'IEEC es desenvolupen a l'Àrea de Promoció del Sector Espacial de Catalunya (APEC).

L'APEC contribueix en els diversos pilars de l'estratègia d'espai de la Generalitat de Catalunya i executa activitats de promoció de la R+D+I, promoció i retenció de talent, construcció d'infraestructures clau tant espacials com terrestres, i de desenvolupament de serveis i aplicacions destinats a l'administració i a sectors industrials.

Té com a objectiu fer créixer l'ecosistema a partir de reforçar les capacitats nacionals a mitjà i llarg termini, i promoure la col·laboració internacional, sempre mantenint el compromís de l'entitat amb els criteris de responsabilitat social.L'APEC engloba les activitats dutes a terme a través de tres oficines destinades a la promoció d'infraestructures, a la promoció de la indústria i els serveis vinculats al sector espacial, i a la promoció de la recerca i la innovació.

Alhora, desenvolupa funcions per a la promoció del talent i pel posicionament de la tecnologia i les dades espacials i la difusió dels beneficis que aquestes produeixen en la resolució dels reptes de la societat.

L'APEC també col·labora estretament amb altres equips de l'IEEC per assolir els seus objectius i manté una coordinació activa amb la Secretaria de Polítiques Digitals de la Generalitat de Catalunya pel desplegament dels diferents programes i activitats emmarcats dins l'estratègia Catalunya Espai ****, així com amb els altres actors vinculats amb l'estratègia.

Més enllà dels programes mateixos, l'APEC cobreix igualment els seus objectius participant en congressos internacionals, col·laborant en projectes amb l'Agència Espacial Espanyola i altres entitats espacials a nivell mundial, contribuint en grups de treball i consorcis, etc.Descripció de la feinaLlocs de treball a cobrir: 1.Gestor/a per donar suport a les activitats de direcció de l'APECGrup professional: Grup II.

Personal d'EnginyeriaCategoria professional: Enginyer/a - Gestor/a de RecercaJornada laboral: 37'5 hores/setmanaUbicació lloc de treball: Seu de l'IEEC - Esteve Terradas 1, Edifici RDIT, Parc Mediterrani de la Tecnologia (PMT), ***** Castelldefels (Barcelona).

FuncionsLes tasques precises assignades a la persona seleccionada per aquesta feina es podran adaptar a qualsevol dels projectes en curs o eixos de treball en funció del seu perfil i de les prioritats de l'equip.

Aquestes tasques inclouran les funcions i responsabilitats següents: Gestió de projectes garantint el compliment de pressupost, terminis i lliurables.Col·laboració transversal pel seguiment de les obligacions legals i pressupostàries en coordinació amb l'Àrea Jurídica i l'Àrea de Gestió de l'IEEC.Col·laboració en la coordinació de les activitats de l'APEC a nivell intern i a nivell extern.Participació en la definició de programes i activitats de les diferents oficines de l'APEC.Creació i manteniment de documentació, incloent informes i estudis tècnics.Gestió de la relació amb stakeholders clau: des d'organismes públics nacionals, estatals i internacionals, empreses i entitats, fins a centres de recerca, operadors d'infraestructures i instal·lacions o usuaris de tecnologies i dades espacials.Col·laboració en el disseny de materials de suport i propostes per posicionar les activitats de l'APEC.Representació de l'IEEC en fòrums, conferències i esdeveniments.Requisits de participació i criteri de seleccióDurant el procés de selecció es valoraran els criteris següents:Titulació mínima amb nivell equivalent de màster, llicenciatura o enginyeria superior, en àmbits tècnics (aeroespacial, telecomunicacions, industrial, mecànica, electrònica, etc.), de ciències (física, matemàtiques, ambientals, geologia, topografia, etc.) o similar.

Experiència i competències: Coneixements i competències en gestió de projectes tècnics relacionats amb el sector espacial o similar.

Experiència professional de més de 3 anys.Idiomes: anglès a nivell professional (equivalent al nivell B2 o superior).

Desplaçament: Carnet de cotxe.Es consideraran mèrits desitjables els aspectes següents (màxim 20 punts):Experiència en tasques similars en gestió de projectes, preferiblement en el sector espacial (fins a 3 punts).

Experiència professional relacionada amb infraestructures terrestres i espacials, serveis i aplicacions basats en tecnologies espacials, recerca i innovació tecnològica espacial (fins a 3 punts).

Coneixement del sector espacial a nivell nacional i internacional (fins a 3 punts).

Coneixement dels procediments de contractació pública i gestió econòmica de projectes (fins a 3 punt).

Organització i atenció al detall: capacitat per planificar, prioritzar i executar tasques amb precisió i rigor (fins a 2 punts).

Visió global i anticipació: capacitat per coordinar múltiples activitats tècniques simultàniament i identificar riscos o oportunitats (fins a 2 punts).

Proactivitat i innovació: capacitat per aportar idees i propostes per millorar processos, solucions tècniques i eficiència operativa (fins a 1 punt).

Aprenentatge i adaptabilitat: dinamisme, curiositat i disposició per adquirir noves competències i adaptar-se a tecnologies i processos emergents (fins a 1 punt).

Capacitat de comunicació (oral i escrita) fluida, efectiva, col·laborativa i constructiva, contribuint a l'esperit d'equip i en un context internacional (fins a 1 punts).

Idiomes: nivell oral i escrit de la llengua catalana i castellana natius o amb competència professional completa (equivalent al nivell C1 o equivalent) (fins a 1 punt).

Contacte i terminiLes persones interessades que reuneixin els requisits establerts poden trametre una carta de motivació, juntament amb el currículum personal en format PDF, signat a mode de certificació, on consti una breu descripció de les tasques realitzades en els llocs ocupats anteriorment, a l'adreça de correu electrònic: ****** tot indicant la referència "cognoms i nom – OF_IEEC_244_****" a l'assumpte del correu.La recepció de candidatures finalitzarà el dia 05/06/2026.Les sol·licituds que no compleixin els requisits, que no estiguin incloses dins els supòsits previstos o que no es tramitin d'acord amb el procediment establert no es tindran en compte.Procediment de seleccióInicialment, es durà a terme una verificació dels requisits de participació i es farà una primera selecció de persones candidates en base a la documentació aportada.

Seguidament, i si s'estima necessari, les persones candidates preseleccionades podran ser convocades a una entrevista personal per ampliar la informació detallada en el currículum i valorar aspectes relacionats amb les competències professionals.La comissió de valoració del present procediment estarà conformada per:a) Director de l'APEC de l'IEEC o persona en qui delegui.b) Cap de l'Oficina d'Infraestructures de l'APEC o persona en qui delegui.b) Cap de l'Oficina de Desenvolupament i Innovació de l'APEC o persona en qui delegui.c) Cap de l'Oficina de Promoció i Serveis Industrials de l'APEC o persona en qui delegui.Les persones interessades han de disposar dels originals dels certificats acreditatius de la formació i de l'experiència laboral esmentades en el currículum i els han de presentar en cas que els siguin requerits.En compliment de la LISMI, l'IEEC, contemplarà la reserva per a les persones candidates que acreditin una discapacitat igual o superior al 33%, sempre que siguin idònies pel desenvolupament de les funcions a cobrir.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Company Description

Inetum is a global leader in IT services, dedicated to delivering innovative solutions to our clients. We are committed to fostering a dynamic and inclusive work environment that values diversity, where creativity and collaboration can thrive. We are present in 19 countries and have more than 28,000 employees worldwide.

If you are looking for a dynamic, innovative, and technology-driven company, Inetum is the place for you! Come be part of Inetum!

Job Description


Identify business problems and propose data-driven solutions to support decision-making and growth
Retrieve, clean, prepare, and analyze complex datasets from multiple sources
Design and implement analyses using statistical methods and data models
Develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations following best practices (Tableau, Power BI)
Build automated tools and solutions to deliver recurring insights efficiently
Contribute to data feed industrialization, ETL pipelines, and data warehousing processes
Translate business requirements into technical specifications for the development team
Communicate complex analytical results clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Collaborate closely with traders, sales teams, business sponsors, and technical teams


Qualifications


Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field
Professional experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau and/or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis using programming languages, preferably Python (Pandas)
Strong ability to analyze, synthesize, and simplify complex information
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus
Familiarity with Big Data technologies such as PySpark, Presto, and Airflow is a plus
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus
Fluent in English

Title: Data Analyst & Data Visualization Specialist
Description:
Company Description

Inetum is a global leader in IT services, dedicated to providing innovative solutions to our clients. We are committed to fostering a dynamic, inclusive workplace that values diversity, where creativity and collaboration thrive. We operate in 19 countries with more than 28,000 employees worldwide.

If you are looking for a dynamic, innovative, and technology-driven company, Inetum is the place for you! Come be Inetum!

Job Description

We are looking for a Data Analyst with strong skills in Data Science, ETL, and Data Visualization to support cybersecurity risk management activities.

The selected candidate will lead end‑to‑end dashboard projects, from gathering requirements to developing data models and delivering analytical insights. This role involves close collaboration with technical and business teams to transform data into meaningful, actionable information.

Main Responsibilities


Understand business requirements and define key KPIs related to cybersecurity risk management.
Collect, clean, and transform datasets using ETL tools to build effective data models.
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports, ensuring data accuracy and availability.
Use data processing and analysis platforms, applying statistical techniques when needed.
Leverage machine learning algorithms to uncover additional insights and identify correlations between indicators.
Present findings to stakeholders clearly, providing recommendations to improve dashboards and reporting.
Monitor data quality and continuously improve data models and processes.
Identify problems proactively and propose effective solutions.
Collaborate with team members across different locations to ensure smooth project execution.
Manage multiple projects simultaneously, demonstrating autonomy and strong time‑management skills.


Qualifications


A bachelor's degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, or a related subject.
3+ years of experience as data Analyst, developing Dashboard and DataViz
Knowledge of algorithms is a plus and will enable the candidate to be a force for proposing useful content related to cyber security risk management.
Expertise with POWERBI, TABLEAU, DATAIKU, PostgreSQL and ETL tools (e.g., TABLEAU Certified Data Analyst, DATAIKU Advanced Designer Certificate)
Experience with other data visualization tools such as POWERBI
Basic Python skills
English – Mastery

Title: Data Analyst (Entry-Level / Early Career) - Lisbon
Description:
Unison is a telecom and renewables infrastructure investment firm funded in partnership with Ardian - the global investment house based in Paris with over $150 billion in assets under management. Unison commenced operations in 2003 and today invests in real estate and infrastructure hosting wireless and renewables tenants in the United States and Europe. The Company is expanding rapidly and looking for talented, entrepreneurial team members to grow with us.

Location: Lisbon, Portugal (on-site or hybrid)

Contract: Initial 6‑month contract with strong potential to become permanent

Salary: Competitive (aligned with Lisbon market)

About Unison

Unison is a telecom and renewable infrastructure investment firm, funded in partnership with Ardian, a leading global investment house headquartered in Paris with over $150 billion in assets under management.

Founded in 2003, Unison invests in real estate and infrastructure that support wireless networks and renewable energy across the United States and Europe. As part of our continued European expansion, we are growing our team in Lisbon and are looking for talented, motivated individuals to join us.

The Role

We are looking for a detail-oriented and organised Data Researcher to support our UK market operations from Lisbon.

This opportunity is well suited to recent graduates, early-career professionals, career changers, or those returning to work. If you enjoy online research, working with structured data, and communicating clearly, this role offers a strong entry point into data-focused work. No technical or programming background is required, and full training will be provided.

Key Responsibilities

Research and validate information from public and online sources

Maintain and update structured datasets with accuracy and consistency

Communicate with UK-based external stakeholders via phone and email

Follow established processes and quality guidelines

Support the team in meeting regular targets and deadlines

What We’re Looking For

Fluent English (written and spoken) is essential

Strong attention to detail and accuracy

Comfortable using computers and learning new tools quickly

Clear communication skills and professional approach

Well-organised, reliable, and proactive mindset

Positive attitude and willingness to learn

Nice to have:

Familiarity with the UK Land Registry system

Experience with GIS or mapping tools

French language skills are a plus given our European context

Who This Role Is Ideal For

Candidates with experience in customer service, administration, or market research

Individuals looking to transition into data or research roles

Entry-level candidates interested in data quality and analysis

What We Offer

Full onboarding and structured training

A supportive and collaborative international team environment

Exposure to UK and French market operations

Clear processes and expectations

A strong entry point into data and research roles within infrastructure investment

Opportunity to move into a permanent position

Eligibility

Applicants must have the right to live and work in Portugal

Candidates must be based in or willing to relocate to Lisbon

Ability to work in an English-speaking environment with UK stakeholders

How To Apply

Please submit a one-page CV and a short cover letter in English

Maintain and update structured datasets with accuracy and consistency

Communicate with UK-based external stakeholders via phone and email

Follow established processes and quality guidelines

Support the team in meeting regular targets and deadlines

What We’re Looking For

Fluent English (written and spoken) is essential

Strong attention to detail and accuracy

Comfortable using computers and learning new tools quickly

Clear communication skills and professional approach

Well-organised, reliable, and proactive mindset

Positive attitude and willingness to learn

Nice to have:

Familiarity with the UK Land Registry system

Experience with GIS or mapping tools

French language skills are a plus given our European context

Who This Role Is Ideal For

Candidates with experience in customer service, administration, or market research

Individuals looking to transition into data or research roles

Entry-level candidates interested in data quality and analysis

What We Offer

Full onboarding and structured training

A supportive and collaborative international team environment

Exposure to UK and French market operations

Clear processes and expectations

A strong entry point into data and research roles within infrastructure investment

Opportunity to move into a permanent position

Applicants must have the right to live and work in Portugal

Please submit a one-page CV and a short cover letter in English

Inetum is a global leader in IT services, dedicated to providing innovative solutions to our clients. We are committed to fostering a dynamic, inclusive workplace that values diversity, where creativity and collaboration thrive. We operate in 19 countries with more than 28,000 employees worldwide.

Experience with ETL pipelines and data modeling
High level of autonomy and sense of responsibility

Good communication skills and ability to work with multiple stakeholders
Results-oriented mindset with a focus on continuous improvement

Title: Business Insights Analyst
Description:
This is a remote role.

About Us

Reachdesk is the first truly end-to-end global SaaS gifting and swag platform, helping B2B companies create meaningful connections with prospects, customers, and employees: while driving measurable ROI.

We believe gifting is more than just a transaction: it’s about building genuine human connections that fuel business growth. Whether you’re engaging prospects, celebrating customers, or recognizing employees, our platform makes it easy to deliver personalized gifts, branded swag, and unforgettable event & brand experiences at scale through automated sourcing, storage, and global delivery.

Trusted by many of the most recognized names in technology and SaaS, Reachdesk integrates effortlessly with your marketing, sales, and HRIS tech stack and is powered by a world-class team with hubs in New York, London, and Lisbon.

We’re growing, and we’re currently looking for a Business Insights Analyst to join our Business Systems & Operations team.

The Role

This is a role for someone energised by the connections between how the business sells, how it serves customers, how it runs operationally and makes money, and who wants to use AI to make those connections faster and clearer than ever.

Every function at Reachdesk sees a different slice of the company. You'll be the person who sits across all of them, using available data and reporting outputs from relevant systems, helping leaders apply consistent commercial and operational definitions when interpreting business performance, and helping leaders see the picture none of them could have produced alone.

Your Responsibilities


Partner with leaders across Sales, Customer Success, Marketing, Finance and Operations to understand the decisions they're each trying to make.
Work with available data from commercial, finance and operational systems to produce insights that connect different views of the business.
Use AI to compress synthesis from days into hours, and to keep our framing of key metrics consistent across functions.
Tell the story behind the numbers in a way that lands with whoever's in the room, commercial, financial or operational.
Create lightweight summaries and repeatable insight outputs so teams can keep answering their own follow-on questions.
Experiment with AI-assisted insight workflows, prompt patterns, evals and lightweight automations, and share what works across the team.


About You


2–4 years in an analyst, insights, strategy or consulting role with exposure to more than one function.
Fluency in at least two of: commercial (revenue, retention, GTM), financial (P&L, unit economics, FP&A), operational (throughput, fulfilment, cycle time).
A genuine AI-native working style; you already use LLMs as a core part of how you think, and you're excited to keep pushing.
Strong written and verbal storytelling; you can adapt your framing for a CRO, a CFO or a Head of Operations without missing a beat.
Comfort working with spreadsheets and reporting tools. Working knowledge of SQL is useful but not essential.


Preferred Expertise


B2B SaaS background.
Familiarity with NetSuite, Salesforce, or the corporate gifting and direct mail space.
Light experience with experiment design, attribution, pricing or operational throughput modeling.


Why Join Us


A team that genuinely treats AI as a leverage tool, not a buzzword.
The closest thing in the company to a true cross-functional vantage point; you'll see how every part of Reachdesk fits together.
You’ll work alongside a small, senior Business Systems & Operations team focused on strategic, cross-functional work.


The Benefits


Competitive salary with generous stock options
Flexible PTO that increases the longer you’re with us, plus extra flexibility for life’s unexpected moments
A genuinely collaborative culture with innovative, fun, and ambitious people


Privacy Policy: We process personal information submitted as part of your application in accordance with the Reachdesk Candidate Privacy Notice. Please review Reachdesk’s Privacy Policy here: www.reachdesk.com/candidate-privacy-policy

Collects, cleans, and organizes data from various sources.

Excellent communication skills.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Engineered in Berlin. Driving the Future of Europe.



At AUTODOC, we are proud to be a homegrown European success story. Founded in Berlin in 2008 and still headquartered in the heart of the continent today, we have evolved from a local specialist into Europe’s leading online platform for the automotive aftermarket.

Our strength lies in our unique identity: we are a digital-first, e-commerce powerhouse with strong German roots and an expansive reach across 27 European countries. This continental focus allows us to blend our heritage with high-tech innovation, staying agile and closely connected to the markets we serve.



Today, our team of more than 5,500 professionals from over 50 different countries is redefining mobility through cutting-edge technology. Bring your talent and expertise to a company that is building a sustainable, tech-driven future for drivers everywhere.



Come join us and impact the future of AUTODOC!





Job Description



The Data Analyst is responsible for identifying opportunities for growth and improvement, and gather, analyze, and interpret data to help the organization to make better decisions. The Data Analyst also work closely with cross-functional teams to ensure that we meet the needs of customers, and drive sales and profitability.



Responsibilities:



Gather and understand business needs and objectives.
Identify and interpret patterns, trends, insights, business metrics from data
Create data-complex dashboards with an optimized data structure and scaling perspective
Assist in planning, forecasting and AB testing processes.
Work with various product and business stakeholders to develop data-driven insights.
Partner with the Data Engineering team to define and build reliable data sources.
Identify new opportunities for the use of data across the company.


Qualifications


Proficiency in SQL, Python/R, Power BI / Tableau, and other data analysis tools
4+ years of experience in data, product analytics, data science or business intelligence, e-commerce experience is a plus
University degree in Data Analytics, Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Finance, Economics, or a related field
Proficiency in statistical analysis methods, data modeling, hypothesis testing, AB testing
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Fluent English
Good communication skills, ability to communicate to both technical and non-technical audiences
Applying expertise in analysis to see beyond the numbers and provide clear insights


Additional Information



What do we offer?



Competitive salaries based on your professional experience
Fast growing international company with stable employment
Annual vacation of 25 working days and 1 additional day off on your birthday
Day off on Municipality day and Carnaval
Meal Allowance (8 euros per working day)
Fidelidade Healthcare Plan (Comprehensive health coverage with the option to include your partner and children at discounted rates).
Mental Wellbeing Program through the OpenUp platform
AUTODOC corporate discount for purchasing car parts at special rates for personal use
Exclusive retail discounts via our ‘Benefits at Work’ portal
Learning & Development (over 650 courses on soft and hard skills on our e-learning platform)
Free English and German language classes (after probation period)
Flexible working hours and hybrid work





Join us today and let’s create a success story together!

Title: Data Analyst Trainee - Real Assets
Description:
About The Job


Data is a strategic asset for BNP Paribas. It plays a fundamental role in the analysis of the teams’ performance and evolution ultimately allowing to become more efficient and to contribute to the sustainability of the business model and the accurate selection of the bank’s assets. To be in control of data is crucial to enhance risk management, to reach right credit decisions and bring added value to the business
Based in Lisbon, the Trainee will join a team of professionals who are known for their sectorial knowledge and support the team


Your Main Activities Are


Input missing data on credit files for presentation to the Credit Committee
Ensure accuracy of credit data
Ensure consistency of data between different credit files
Communicate with the CPM Credit Analyst Teams aiming to get updates on progress of credit files
Ensure proper understanding of credit files to identify structured data to be inputted
Create and maintain standard procedures for data input (user guides)
Cooperate and communicate with the teams in charge of the golden source systems to ensure correct data in the source system
Produce periodic reports based on data collected and captured, providing clear analysis and interpretation


Profile And Skills To Success


Bachelor’s Degree in Economics, Accounting or Management
Detail oriented with deep interest in data
Good knowledge of Excel
Good written/verbal communication skills in English (mandatory)
Ability to collaborate / teamwork
Efficient organizational and planning capacities
Self-starter, good initiative and flexible


About The Team


The Real Assets team includes a Coverage Relationship & Origination team, a Transaction & Execution team and a Credit Portfolio Management Team (CPM) spread across several countries serving the sector client in EMEA. These teams are split across Transportation (Shipping & Offshore, Aviation, Rail), Real Estate, Energy, Recourses & Infrastructure (ERI), Export, Leasing & Alternative Structuring. This transversality aims to provide a common training path, opportunities to explore other sectors, more efficiency and greater mobility


Why joining BNP Paribas?


Leading banking institution


BNP Paribas is the European Union’s leading bank, and key player in international banking. It operates in 63 countries and has nearly 183.000 employees, including more than 146.000 in Europe.


Our presence in Portugal


In Portugal since 1985, BNP Paribas today has more than 8.700 employees, distributed across the Group's 10 business entities established in the country. Its presence also extends to 11 excellence centers providing value-added services to various countries where the BNP Paribas Group also operates.


International reach


Thanks To Its International Presence And Regular And Close Collaboration Among Its Different Entities, BNP Paribas Has The Resources To Support All Clients With Financing, Investment, Savings And Protection Solutions That Help Make Their Projects a Success. BNP Paribas Holds Key Positions In Its Three Core Operating Divisions


Retail Banking, a division that brings together all of the Group’s retail activities and specialised business lines;
Investment & Protection Services that include specialised businesses offering a wide range of savings, investment and protection services;
Corporate & Institutional Banking division that offers tailored financial solutions for corporate and institutional clients.
Diversity and Inclusion commitment


BNP Paribas is an equal opportunity employer and proud to provide equal employment opportunity to all job seekers. We are actively committed to ensuring that no individual is discriminated against on the grounds of age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage or civil partnership status, pregnancy and maternity/paternity, race, religion or belief, sex or sexual orientation. Equity and diversity are at the core of our recruitment policy because we believe that they foster creativity and efficiency, which in turn increase performance and productivity. We strive to reflect the society we live in, while keeping with the image of our clients.


Commitment towards work/life balance


At BNP Paribas we care about our employees wellbeing and promote a culture of good integration between work and rest. We believe our employees have rich personal lives outside of work, being fundamental to be disconnected from work to recharge both physically and mentally. Only through this balance we may all be at our best while working.


Remote Working Conditions


At BNP Paribas, we embrace a Smart Working framework based on trust, autonomy and collaboration. Within this framework, eligible employees can benefit from flexible remote working modalities adapted to our hybrid working environment. To guarantee a comfortable and efficient working set-up, eligible employees are provided with both the office and home equipment, are entitled to an equipment allowance and can benefit from exclusive partnerships to purchase additional equipment at reduced prices.

To find out more on why you should join BNP Paribas visit https://bnpp.lk/why-BNP-Paribas-Portugal


Please note that only applications submitted in English will be considered.
In case you are selected for this role, further documentation will be requested to support your hiring process.

Title: Senior Data Analyst - CRM (m/f/d)
Description:
At Flix, we offer a dynamic work environment with competitive pay, strong growth opportunities, and a tech-driven approach to making travel more accessible, sustainable, and affordable. 

As a Senior Data Analyst (m/f/d), you will join our global CRM team. This is an important role for CRM at Flix as we expand our capabilities, redefine our communication strategy and constantly optimize for maximum growth, as well as scalability and efficiency. We support global market growth with our marketing activities to increase repeated purchases and prevent churn. We always aim for cutting-edge technologies and a high level of innovation. By joining our team, you will be a crucial part of driving the team forward, developing our brands and customer communication throughout the globe.

About The Role


Own CRM analytics and reporting across 45+ markets, delivering clear visibility into performance, customer behavior, and commercial impact
Build and maintain Power BI dashboards providing real-time tracking of CRM performance, targets, and business trends
Deliver actionable insights on campaign performance, drivers of customer behavior, value-generating segments, and cross-market differences
Develop scalable, automated reporting solutions alongside deep-dive analyses and ad hoc business investigations
Monitor and optimize key CRM KPIs (engagement, conversion, retention, reactivation, communication effectiveness) and customer lifecycle performance
Partner with CRM, data, and product teams to improve segmentation, personalization, experimentation, tracking quality, and data reliability
Evaluate A/B tests and proactively identify trends, anomalies, risks, and opportunities, presenting clear recommendations to senior stakeholders


About You


5+ years of experience in data analytics or BI, ideally within CRM, marketing, or e-commerce environments
Proven track record of building dashboards and automated reporting solutions for non-technical stakeholders
Strong SQL expertise (CTEs, window functions, multi-table joins) in a modern cloud data warehouse, preferably Snowflake
Advanced Power BI skills including data modeling, DAX, and best-practice dashboard design and visualization
Solid understanding of marketing and CRM KPIs (conversion, engagement, attribution, customer lifetime value)

Title: Data Analyst - Operational and Permanent Control - Lisbon or Porto
Description:
About The Job

The Operational and Permanent Control (OPC) Data Analyst plays a key role in Global Banking Chief Conduct and Control Officer (GB CCCO) Office Front-to-Ops missions and worldwide mandate to support on the identification and assessment of non-financial risks. It ensures the enforcement and efficiency of the control framework and to promote an adequate risk management culture.

Your Main Activities Are


Data monitoring and report through the creation of business-facing dashboards and high impact data visualizations and analysis
Understand the stakeholders strategic needs
Create descriptive, predictive, and quantitative analyses addressing key business priorities and supporting the decision making
Support and monitor the run of these activities of data analysis, reporting and data mining
Supporting on the analysis of risk identification and assessment ensuring its validation by the relevant management level and consistency with the defined risk tolerance
Monitor and Report Recommendations, Permanent Control Actions, Self-identified action plans and follow-up of remediation plans
Centralize and automate reports production, gathering major operational risks indicators (automated KPI and KRI dashboards)
Automate relevant sampling creation, control execution and reporting
Perform periodic and ad-hoc reporting to the appropriate level of management and to independent control functions
Show ability to take ownership of specific OPC activity streams and topics being able to deliver the full and exhaustive actions taken
Whenever needed, support on management of procedures, control execution, incidents monitoring and any other CCCO GB related tasks
Provide visibility on performance and activities of CCCO GB within the team or any other stakeholder


Profile and Skills to Success


Bachelor's or Master´s Degree in Engineering, Data Analytics, or Finance related fields
Fluency in English
Up to 2 years of experience in data analytics
Experience using Microsoft Office Tools, particularly Excel
Proficiency in Power BI, SQL
Attention to detail and creative thinking
Ability to synthetize
Ability to deliver results


Why joining BNP Paribas?


Leading banking institution


Leading player in banking and financial services in Europe, BNP Paribas operates in 64 countries and has nearly 178,000 employees, including more than 144,000 in Europe


International reach


The Group holds key positions in several areas of banking and financial services that are structured around three operating divisions:


Corporate & Institutional Banking (CIB) connects companies needing financing and institutional clients looking for investment opportunities division that offers tailored financial solutions for corporate and institutional clients
Commercial, Personal Banking & Services (CPBS) brings together all our commercial banks and several specialised businesses heard
Investment & Protection Services (IPS) combines a unique continuum of products and services across protection, saving, investment and real estate including specialised businesses offering a wide range of savings, investment and protection services
Our presence in Portugal


In Portugal since 1985, BNP Paribas today has more than 9.220 employees, distributed across the Group's 10 business entities established in the country. Its presence also extends to 11 centres of expertise providing value-added services to various countries where the BNP Paribas Group also operates.


Diversity and Inclusion commitment


BNP Paribas ´ambition Is To Cultivate An Open And Responsive Environment For All That Encourages Collaboration And Interaction. We Demonstrate Our Commitment By Actively Contributing To The Group’s Diversity And Inclusion Strategy And Goals, In Line With The United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. As Part Of Our Local Corporate And Social Responsibility – Diversity & Inclusion Strategy, BNP Paribas Portugal Is Committed To


Promote a fair and unbiased recruitment process and offer professional development opportunities to all employees
Celebrate diversity and advocate for inclusion, both externally and internally, encouraging employee’s participation, creating space for different voices to be heard.


To foster the effort of BNP Paribas Portugal, multiple initiatives and events take place throughout the year where our people can find out more on the importance of diversity inclusion at the workplace and in our society.


Remote Working Conditions


At BNP Paribas, we embrace a Smart Working framework based on trust, autonomy and collaboration. Within this framework, eligible employees can benefit from flexible remote working options adapted to our hybrid working environment. To ensure a comfortable and efficient working setup, eligible employees are provided with both office and home equipment, are entitled to an equipment allowance and can benefit from exclusive partnerships to purchase additional items at reduced prices.


Commitment towards work/life balance


Supporting employees in a hybrid way of working while providing them with the means to maintain a work-life balance is an essential dimension of our Smart Working program. At BNP Paribas, we care about our employees’ wellbeing and promote a culture of good integration between work and personal life.

To find out more on why you should join BNP Paribas please read our Employee Value Proposition and our Career path page.


Please note that only applications submitted in English will be considered.
In case you are selected for this role, further documentation will be requested to support your hiring process.

Title: Analyst (Portugal)
Description:
About Cleerly

We’re Cleerly – a healthcare company that’s revolutionizing how heart disease is diagnosed, treated, and tracked. We were founded in 2017 by one of the world’s leading cardiologists and are a growing team of world-class engineering, operations, medical affairs, marketing, and sales leaders. We raised $223M in Series C funding in 2022 which has enabled rapid growth and continued support of our mission. In December 2024 we received an additional $106M in a Series C extension funding. Most of our teams work remotely and have access to our offices in Denver, Colorado, New, York, New York, Dallas, Texas, and Lisbon, Portugal with some roles requiring you to be on-site in a location.

Cleerly has created a new standard of care for heart disease through value-based, AI-driven precision diagnostic solutions with the goal of helping prevent heart attacks. Our technology goes beyond traditional measures of heart disease by enabling comprehensive quantification and characterization of atherosclerosis, or plaque buildup, in each of the heart arteries. Cleerly’s solutions are supported by more than a decade of performing some of the world’s largest clinical trials to identify important findings beyond symptoms that increase a person’s risk of heart attacks.

At Cleerly, we collaborate digitally and use a wide variety of systems. Our people use Google Workspace (GMail, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Slides), Slack, Confluence/Jira, and Zoom Video, prior experience in these areas is a plus. Role or department specific technology needs may vary and will be listed as requirements in the job description.

While we are mostly a remote company, travel is required for some team meetings and cross function projects typically once per month or once per quarter, for some roles like sales or external facing roles travel could be up to 90% of the time.

Cleerly is committed to providing safe and effective medical software that meets customer needs and our intended use. The adherence to all applicable regulatory and statutory requirements establishes a clear framework for setting measurable quality objectives. Our commitment to continually improving our products and processes proactively manages risks, ensuring ongoing compliance throughout the entire software lifecycle. Understanding this role's relevance and importance is critical to achieving Cleerly's quality objectives.

About The Opportunity

You will have the opportunity to develop the experience of planning and operationalize daily schedules, in order to accomplish the targets. Work in a different and growing innovative environment that mixes healthcare with AI in a market in expansion. Develop experience of working with a multicultural team local and remote.

Responsibilities


Analyze outputs generated by artificial intelligence from Coronary CTA exams.
Evaluate Coronary CTA exams for image quality.
Perform administrative duties as required.
Participation in continuous improvement projects
Comply with process and quality standards.


Requirements


Medical imaging and radiotherapy university Degree
Availability to work different shifts
High attention to detail
Great interpersonal skills - must be able to work well in a team environment
Desire and ability to learn new technologies - willing to learn
Coronary CTA or 3D post-processing experience is a plus


Salary:

€1460.00 (monthly)

Working at Cleerly takes HEART. Discover our Core Values:


H: Humility- be a servant leader
E: Excellence- deliver world-changing results
A: Accountability- do what you say; expect the same from others
R: Remarkable- inspire & innovate with impact
T: Teamwork- together we win


Don’t meet 100 percent of the qualifications? Apply anyway and help us diversify our candidate pool and workforce. We value experience, whether gained formally or informally on the job or through other experiences. Job duties, activities and responsibilities are subject to change by our company.

OUR COMPANY IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, protected veteran status, religion, physical or mental disability, marital status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, or any other basis protected by law, ordinance, or regulation.

For more information see our Privacy Policy (https://cleerlyhealth.com/privacy-policy). All official emails will come from @cleerlyhealth.com email accounts.

#Cleerly

Title: Business Analyst (Data & BI) - Associate
Description:
Company Description

Our vision is to be the world leader in sports betting and gaming entertainment by creating the most exciting and trusted experience for our customers, revolutionizing the gambling space as we go. We're home to a global family of >25 well-known brands, and with a focus on sustainability and growth, we will transform our sector for our players, ourselves and for the good of entertainment.

This role sits at the core of our Data & BI function, translating data into actionable insights for stakeholders. The Associate Business Analyst will work closely with business teams to understand their needs, extract and analyse data and deliver clear, impactful outputs through dashboards, reporting, and ad-hoc analysis.

The position combines technical data skills with strong business understanding, requiring the ability not only to produce data, but to interpret it, challenge assumptions, and drive meaningful outcomes.

This is the ideal opportunity for someone with a passion for sports to develop their analytical and reporting skills within a fast-paced and competitive environment.

Job Description


Extract, manipulate, and analyse large datasets from Snowflake using SQL, turning data into clear, actionable insights - not just outputs.
Build and maintain dashboards and reporting solutions, ensuring they are scalable, reliable, and widely adopted by the business.
Translate business questions into structured analysis, delivering concise and impactful insights to stakeholders.
Deliver ad-hoc analysis to support decision-making across multiple business areas, with a strong focus on speed and accuracy.
Contribute to the development and maintenance of self-serve analytics, enabling teams to independently access and explore data.
Work closely with Data Engineering and other teams to improve data availability, quality, and usability.
Continuously challenge assumptions, identify opportunities, and proactively propose data-driven solutions to business problems.


Qualifications

Essential:


A degree in Statistics, Economics, Mathematics, Computer Science or related field.
2+ years’ experience with SQL and data analysis.
Proficiency in Excel.
Working knowledge of SQL, with the ability to query and manipulate large datasets.
Fluent level of written and spoken English.
An understanding of statistical analysis techniques.


Desired


Advanced SQL skills
Interest in sports and/or familiarity with the sports betting / iGaming industry
Experience with dbt and GIT
Experience with tools such as Google Analytics, Optimove, or similar marketing/data platforms
Experience with BI and data visualization tools (e.g. Omni, Looker, Power BI, Tableau)
Experience working in digital, data-driven, or iGaming environments
Portuguese and/or Spanish


Competencies/Behaviours

Additional Information


Team player.
Strong interpersonal skills.
Proactivity, hands on approach
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent attention to detail.
Ability to analyse large amounts of data quickly and accurately.
Relentless curiosity: Questions the status quo, continually asks why/why not.
Focused on achieving targets and meeting deadlines.
Takes initiative and can work autonomously.
Show more
HTML:
Company Description

Our vision is to be the world leader in sports betting and gaming entertainment by creating the most exciting and trusted experience for our customers, revolutionizing the gambling space as we go. We're home to a global family of >25 well-known brands, and with a focus on sustainability and growth, we will transform our sector for our players, ourselves and for the good of entertainment.

This role sits at the core of our Data & BI function, translating data into actionable insights for stakeholders. The Associate Business Analyst will work closely with business teams to understand their needs, extract and analyse data and deliver clear, impactful outputs through dashboards, reporting, and ad-hoc analysis.

The position combines technical data skills with strong business understanding, requiring the ability not only to produce data, but to interpret it, challenge assumptions, and drive meaningful outcomes.

This is the ideal opportunity for someone with a passion for sports to develop their analytical and reporting skills within a fast-paced and competitive environment.

Job Description

  • Extract, manipulate, and analyse large datasets from Snowflake using SQL, turning data into clear, actionable insights - not just outputs.
  • Build and maintain dashboards and reporting solutions, ensuring they are scalable, reliable, and widely adopted by the business.
  • Translate business questions into structured analysis, delivering concise and impactful insights to stakeholders.
  • Deliver ad-hoc analysis to support decision-making across multiple business areas, with a strong focus on speed and accuracy.
  • Contribute to the development and maintenance of self-serve analytics, enabling teams to independently access and explore data.
  • Work closely with Data Engineering and other teams to improve data availability, quality, and usability.
  • Continuously challenge assumptions, identify opportunities, and proactively propose data-driven solutions to business problems.

Qualifications

Essential:

  • A degree in Statistics, Economics, Mathematics, Computer Science or related field.
  • 2+ years’ experience with SQL and data analysis.
  • Proficiency in Excel.
  • Working knowledge of SQL, with the ability to query and manipulate large datasets.
  • Fluent level of written and spoken English.
  • An understanding of statistical analysis techniques.

Desired

  • Advanced SQL skills
  • Interest in sports and/or familiarity with the sports betting / iGaming industry
  • Experience with dbt and GIT
  • Experience with tools such as Google Analytics, Optimove, or similar marketing/data platforms
  • Experience with BI and data visualization tools (e.g. Omni, Looker, Power BI, Tableau)
  • Experience working in digital, data-driven, or iGaming environments
  • Portuguese and/or Spanish

Competencies/Behaviours

Additional Information

  • Team player.
  • Strong interpersonal skills.
  • Proactivity, hands on approach
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Excellent attention to detail.
  • Ability to analyse large amounts of data quickly and accurately.
  • Relentless curiosity: Questions the status quo, continually asks why/why not.
  • Focused on achieving targets and meeting deadlines.
  • Takes initiative and can work autonomously.

Title: Analista de dados
Description:
Na Axians, valorizamos o talento, não os rótulos.

Acreditamos numa cultura de inclusão, onde todas as pessoas têm lugar e todas as candidaturas serão consideradas com base no mérito, sem discriminação.

Esta é a tua oportunidade de integrares um grupo internacional, com um projeto que precisa de ti para responder aos desafios da transformação digital.

💻 O CARGO

Procuramos um #TechTalent para desempenhar funções enquanto Analista de Dados para um projeto no setor da saúde.

💡O TEU DIA A DIA


Análise e transformação de dados - Integração, tratamento e transformação de dados para suporte à área de Data Analytics, com foco na criação de relatórios e dashboards
Desenvolvimento e melhoria de relatórios - Criação, manutenção e otimização de relatórios nas áreas de Logística e RH, incluindo melhorias de performance, automatizações e evolução de soluções já existentes.
Suporte à transformação e automatização de processos - Colaboração em iniciativas de melhoria contínua, automatização de reporting e suporte às necessidades do negócio.


O QUE PROCURAMOS


Experiência prévia de pelo menos 3 anos em análise de dados
Conhecimento sólido de SQL, Python e Alteryx
Licença de Alteryx (obrigatório)


⭐ O QUE PODES ESPERAR DE NÓS

Carreira profissional estável

Ao trabalhar com projetos da Axians torna-se uma mais valia a quem queremos oferecer estabilidade, através de uma carreira segura.

Perspetivas de progressão na carreira

Poderá aceder a formações e novas certificações que elevarão ainda mais as suas competências. A avaliação 360° dos managers e peers também permitirão evoluir.

Conciliação entre a área profissional e a familiar

Colaborando com a Axians há abertura para conciliar as exigências laborais com os requisitos familiares, evitando negligenciar cada uma das áreas.

Compensação evolutiva e atrativa

Ao trabalhar com clientes da Axians, terá acesso a prémios de performance, além de um pack salarial atrativo (vencimento base + benefícios). Também tem direito a seguro de saúde e consultas de psicologia gratuitas.

Identifica-se com esta vaga?

Se pensa que esta vaga é a sua cara e tem o #TechTalent que procuramos envie-nos o seu currículo, de preferência em inglês e em formato Europass, para recrutamento.axianspt@axians.comcom o subject "AD.SA.TD".

Saber mais sobre a Axians

Title: Transformation Junior Analyst
Description:
Choosing Capgemini means choosing a company where you will be empowered to shape your career in the way you’d like, where you’ll be supported and inspired by a collaborative community of colleagues around the world, and where you’ll be able to reimagine what’s possible. Join us and help the world’s leading organizations unlock the value of technology and build a more sustainable, more inclusive world.

Your Role

Are you a motivated Junior Analyst looking to start your career in a transformation and regulatory environment? Join our team in Lisbon and support KYC/AML and Client Lifecycle Management teams through incident coordination, training support and clear communication.

In this role, you will:


Support product incident tracking and status update communications for internal KYC tools.
Assist in the creation of training materials and digital learning content.
Maintain knowledge repositories (SharePoint / Teams) with up-to-date documentation.
Collaborate with business, IT and product teams in agile transformation initiatives.
Support stakeholder meetings, workshops and internal communications.


Your Profile


Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Law, Media, Communications or similar (or 1–2 years of relevant trainee/internship experience).
Fluent in English, written and spoken; additional languages are a plus.
Strong communication skills and ability to simplify complex topics.
Good knowledge of Microsoft Office, especially PowerPoint.
Interest in KYC/AML, regulatory processes and transformation projects.
Autonomous, hands-on and eager to learn on the job.
Comfortable working in cross-functional and agile teams.
Experience with media or digital content creation is a strong plus, but not mandatory.


What You'll Love About Working Here


At Capgemini Portugal we have a flexible and dynamic work environment. Flexibility enables a better work-life balance and gives more flexibility to the employee to manage the working hours, as well if he works at the office or remotely, according with the company’s hybrid work policy;
We have local programs that promote people growth, reskill and new skills development (Career Acceleration Programs);
We promote an empowering environment with autonomy and peers' relationships among the top scores of our Monthly Employees' feedback; Next to this, we also offer an attractive compensation package and benefits such as Health and Life insurance, as well as Referral program with bonuses for talent recommendations and other fringe benefits according with our partnerships in force.
Capgemini Portugal is an equal opportunity employer. We promote equality and dignity in all aspects of recruitment and employment, as well as employment offers and promotions made according with competence and ability or performance, respectively.


About Capgemini

Capgemini is a global business and technology transformation partner, helping organizations to accelerate their dual transition to a digital and sustainable world, while creating tangible impact for enterprises and society. It is a responsible and diverse group of 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong over 55-year heritage, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to unlock the value of technology to address the entire breadth of their business needs. It delivers end-to-end services and solutions leveraging strengths from strategy and design to engineering, all fuelled by its market leading capabilities in AI, cloud and data, combined with its deep industry expertise and partner ecosystem. The Group reported 2023 global revenues of €22.5 billion.

Get the future you want | www.capgemini.com

Apply now!

Title: Operate Analyst - Lisboa
Description:
Queres integrar uma das maiores organizações de serviços profissionais do mercado? Junta-te a nós e vais poder aprender mais sobre a área de operações de uma instituição financeira. Aqui, vais potenciar a tua carreira e reforçar as tuas competências.

Vamos reforçar a nossa equipa da zona de Lisboa, com Operate Analysts, que tenham interesse em trabalhar na área de Operações de Serviço de BackOffice e Reporting.

Procuramos profissionais com um forte sentido de responsabilidade e orientação para resultados, e que tenham motivação e flexibilidade para trabalhar em equipa.

Na Deloitte, oferecemos uma experiência profissional que inspira talentos como tu a transformar o mundo como o conhecemos, tornando-o mais humano, eficiente e tecnológico. Estás preparado para te juntar a uma comunidade talentosa e diversa, e criar valor para as nossas pessoas, empresas e sociedade?

Vem iniciar uma viagem incrível pelo universo Deloitte. Estamos à tua espera.

O teu dia a dia e responsabilidades:


Gestão de incidentes de negócio e respetivo seguimento até resolução.
Gestão e controlo de ficheiros relacionados com evolutivos, pedidos de alteração e entregas de informação.
Acompanhamento de ficheiros de vendas, validação de qualidade de dados e deteção de desvios.
Elaboração, atualização e controlo de KPI operacionais e comerciais.
Produção de reporting recorrente e ad hoc para áreas de negócio e gestão.
Suporte analítico com recurso a Power BI e Excel para exploração, modelação e visualização de dados.



És tu quem procuramos?


Experiência prévia em funções de backoffice, reporting, controlo operacional ou análise de dados.
Conhecimentos avançados de Excel, incluindo fórmulas, tabelas dinâmicas, power query e tratamento de grandes volumes de informação.
Domínio de Power BI para modelação de dados, construção de dashboards e análise visual.
Elevada capacidade analítica, atenção ao detalhe e orientação para qualidade.
Boa capacidade de organização, gestão de prioridades e cumprimento de prazos.
Autonomia, proatividade e facilidade de comunicação com equipas multidisciplinares.
Capacidade para transformar informação dispersa em reporting claro, acionável e fiável.



Desenvolve uma carreira à tua medida num contexto de trabalho diverso que permite um desenvolvimento e aprendizagem contínuos através de um plano de desenvolvimento de carreira personalizado (Competências/ oportunidades técnicas e funcionais), adequado ao momento, às tuas necessidades e objetivos

Acesso ilimitado às maiores plataformas de aprendizagem online (Linkedin Learning e Udemy)

Vive uma cultura de união

Uma cultura de inovação e excelência que nos torna líderes e precursores de tendências

Um ambiente amigável, de respeito, confiança, transparência e informalidade

Uma rede para a vida que te faz estar ligado ao melhor conhecimento ao lado de líderes e especialistasUma oportunidade para fazeres parte das nossas comunidades (de bem-estar, desporto, inovação e outros interesses) e para participares ativamente em projetos de responsabilidade social

Sobre a área

O Process Delivery Center presta serviços profissionais em diversos processos de negócio em clientes nacionais e internacionais.

Atualmente os nossos profissionais trabalham nos nossos escritórios do Porto, Lisboa e Viseu.

Fazemos um acompanhamento próximo de todas as nossas pessoas seguindo o lema da firma "You will never work alone". Temos várias iniciativas para o acolhimento de novos profissionais, nomeadamente: Welcome program, Buddy, Career Coach, entre outras. Mas o mais relevante é que terás sempre alguém muito próximo a ajudar-te no dia-a-dia nos projetos. A nossa equipa de gestão é bastante jovem e acessível.

Organizamos eventos formais e informais da área que potenciam este sentimento de união e pertença.

Apostamos no teu futuro de forma proativa sendo que há formação da Firma e especifica da área que te vão ajudar na tua carreira e a evoluir nas tuas skills técnicas e softskills.

Sobre a Deloitte

Na Deloitte nunca trabalharás sozinho. Terás sempre alguém ao teu lado para te inspirar, ajudar e desafiar, enquanto exploras e desenvolves soluções diferenciadoras com impacto nos clientes e na sociedade. Encontrarás um ambiente dinâmico, colaborativo e humano, onde poderás dar o melhor de ti, participar e beneficiar das nossas iniciativas de bem-estar e flexibilidade. Vais integrar uma comunidade de pessoas talentosas, algumas delas referências nacionais e internacionais na sua área de especialidade, que vão acompanhar-te para a vida, manter-te ligado ao melhor conhecimento e embarcar contigo numa jornada de aprendizagem que vai potenciar a tua progressão de carreira.

Em Portugal, somos mais de 5.500 e fazemos parte de uma rede global de firmas membro, com equipas multidisciplinares que combinam competências de negócio com tecnologia. Esta diversidade de perspetivas e a multiplicidade de experiências acumuladas ao longo dos anos, permitem-nos levar o nosso conhecimento para o mundo, antecipar tendências e responder aos mais complexos projetos.

Sabe mais em www.deloitte.com/pt/careers
Show more
