  • Inclusive people policies (sickness, menopause, compassionate and fertility leave)
  • A chance to give back to your local community with 5 paid volunteering days

OUR BEHAVIOURS 🏆

  • Grow others - We fundamentally believe that investing in growing others benefits everyone, whether it's helping them develop hard or soft skills. We want learning and growing to be part of our DNA to help makes us a better team, together.
  • Master it - We are obsessed with our area of expertise and enjoy developing our skills. We rarely take things at face value; we investigate, interrogate, and always look for ‘the why,’ and wherever possible, we use data to find the best solution.
  • Collaborate - We are in it together, for the tough stuff and the celebrations too. To achieve the best results, we need expertise from all areas of the organisation, and we wholeheartedly welcome diverse thinking.
  • Adapt - We work fluidly, adapting to new information and the evolving environment while staying committed to our goals. Innovation and experimentation fuel our projects and we’re never afraid to pivot.
  • Deliver - Our focus is always on the end result; we value outcomes over activity. We collaborate to deliver work at speed without dropping any of our other behaviours.

We believe in talented and diverse teams that reflect the diversity of our customers and the communities in which we operate. Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences. We lay out the above requirements to guide applicants to the experiences that we believe will allow you to be successful in the role. If you don’t meet them all, please consider applying if you think you can still perform the role as described.

Hostelworld is the world’s leading Social Travel platform - a social network powered OTA built to help travellers find their people.

Our mission is simple: we connect like‑minded travellers so they can create unforgettable moments together. Most people go hostelling to meet others, and we’ve built a global ecosystem that makes those connections happen.

Since launching our social network in 2022, we’ve welcomed more than 2.6 million social members who aren’t just booking trips — they’re messaging, meeting up, sharing stories and becoming passionate advocates for our brand. This vibrant community generates rich, authentic content and powerful social proof that fuels our growth.

Our focus on helping travellers find people to hang out with has created a new category in travel. We’re not a traditional OTA: our mobile‑native social features create a sticky, high‑engagement experience, and the proprietary data generated by our community gives us a competitive advantage that is extremely hard to replicate. This “social flywheel” helps us understand traveller behaviour, personalise experiences and deliver a differentiated product that keeps users coming back.

Founded in 1999 and headquartered in Ireland, we are approximately 270 employees strong, with hostel partners in more than 180 countries. Alongside our focus on connection, we are committed to building a better world — from advancing sustainability in the hostelling sector to giving travellers the tools to make more informed and climate‑conscious choices.

At Hostelworld, we’re shaping the future of travel by putting people, community and shared experiences at the heart of everything we do.

Title: Analytics Engineer (BI)
Description:
Company Description

Sports betting, gaming and interactive entertainment is changing, and we’re leading that change. By putting people first. By placing exciting, engaging and entertaining experiences at more fingertips than ever before. We're pulling into pole position by pushing boundaries further. With innovation. With technology. But most importantly, with people like you. Because when you join Entain, it’s your game. So let’s win together.

Job Description

This role sits at the core of our Data & BI function, translating data into actionable insights for stakeholders. The Associate Business Analyst will work closely with business teams to understand their needs, extract and analyse data and deliver clear, impactful outputs through dashboards, reporting, and ad-hoc analysis.

The position combines technical data skills with strong business understanding, requiring the ability not only to produce data, but to interpret it, challenge assumptions, and drive meaningful outcomes.

This is the ideal opportunity for someone with a passion for sports to develop their analytical and reporting skills within a fast-paced and competitive environment.


Extract, manipulate, and analyse large datasets from Snowflake using SQL, turning data into clear, actionable insights - not just outputs.
Design, build, and maintain dbt models for the analytics semantic layer, ensuring data consistency, documentation, and reusability.
Build and maintain dashboards and reporting solutions, ensuring they are scalable, reliable, and widely adopted by the business.
Translate business questions into structured analysis, delivering concise and impactful insights to stakeholders.
Deliver ad-hoc analysis to support decision-making across multiple business areas, with a strong focus on speed and accuracy.
Contribute to the development and maintenance of self-serve analytics, enabling teams to independently access and explore data.
Apply version control best practices (Git) to all analytical work, including dbt models and documentation.
Support team members in dbt, Git, and data modelling practices through day-to-day collaboration and knowledge sharing.
Work closely with Data Engineering and other teams to improve data availability, quality, and usability.
Continuously challenge assumptions, identify opportunities, and proactively propose data-driven solutions to business problems.


Qualifications

Essential:


A degree in Statistics, Economics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or related field.
Demonstrable experience with SQL and data analysis.
Advanced SQL skills, with the ability to query, optimise, and model large datasets in Snowflake or similar cloud data warehouses.
Hands-on experience with dbt (building, testing, and documenting models).
Working knowledge of Git and version control workflows.
Understanding of data modelling principles (star schema, dimensional modelling).
Proficiency in Excel. Fluent level of written and spoken English.


Desired


Experience with BI and data visualisation tools (e.g. Omni, Looker, Power BI, Tableau).
Interest in sports and/or familiarity with the sports betting / iGaming industry.
An understanding of statistical analysis techniques.
Experience working in digital, data-driven, or iGaming environments.
Experience with tools such as Google Analytics, Optimove, or similar marketing/data platforms
Portuguese and/or Spanish


Additional Information

At Entain, we know that signing top players requires a great starting package, and plenty of support to inspire peak performance. Join us, and a competitive salary is just the beginning.

Depending on your role and location, you can expect to receive benefits like


A regular bonus
Healthcare support
A stake in our success through our ShareSave scheme
Great development opportunities
Wellbeing support, and so much more.


And outside of this, you’ll have the chance to turn recognition from leaders and colleagues into amazing prizes, join a winning team of talented people and be a part of an inclusive and supporting community where everyone is celebrated for being themselves.

Should you need any adjustments or accommodations to the recruitment process, at either application or interview, please contact us.

Title: Gen AI Trainee Program
Description:
Country: Portugal

COMEÇA AQUI

O Santander (www.santander.com) está a evoluir de uma marca global de grande impacto para uma organização orientada pela tecnologia, e as nossas pessoas estão no centro desta jornada.

Juntos, estamos a liderar uma transformação centrada no cliente, que valoriza o pensamento crítico, a inovação e a coragem de desafiar o que é possível.

Esta é mais do que uma mudança estratégica. É uma oportunidade para pessoas motivadas crescerem, aprenderem e fazerem uma diferença real.

A nossa missão é contribuir para que mais pessoas e empresas prosperem. Adotámos uma cultura de risco sólida, e todas as nossas pessoas, em todos os níveis, têm um papel ativo e responsável na gestão de riscos.

O TEU IMPACTO

O Santander está à procura de um(a) estagiário/a de GenAI para Lisboa.

Procuramos talento com interesse em desenvolvimento de software e inteligência artificial para integrar a nossa equipa de GenAI. O foco será o desenvolvimento de aplicações e microserviços que utilizam modelos de AI generativa pré-treinados (como os da OpenAI) para resolver desafios reais de negócio. Esta é uma excelente oportunidade para aplicar conhecimentos técnicos em projetos inovadores com impacto direto.

Estamos a redefinir a forma como trabalhamos através da inovação, da tecnologia de ponta, da colaboração e da liberdade para explorar novas ideias.

Para ter sucesso nesta função, serás responsável por:


Desenvolver aplicações e microserviços que integrem modelos de GenAI via APIs.
Criar e manter pipelines de dados e serviços backend escaláveis.
Trabalhar com equipas multidisciplinares para identificar casos de uso e implementar soluções baseadas em IA.
Garantir boas práticas de engenharia de software, incluindo testes, versionamento e integração contínua.
Explorar novas ferramentas e abordagens para melhorar a eficiência e a qualidade das soluções desenvolvidas.


O TEU CONTRIBUTO

As nossas pessoas são a nossa maior força. Cada indivíduo contribui com perspetivas únicas que nos tornam mais fortes como equipa e como organização.

Capacitamos as equipas para irem mais além, valorizando quem são e potenciando o que trazem consigo.

Os seguintes requisitos representam os conhecimentos, competências e capacidades essenciais para o sucesso nesta função. Serão consideradas adaptações razoáveis para permitir que pessoas com deficiência possam desempenhar as funções essenciais.

FORMAÇÃO ACADÉMICA


Formação em áreas de STEM. (obrigatório)


IDIOMAS


Português (obrigatório)
Inglês (preferencial)
Espanhol (preferencial)


Hard Skills


Conhecimentos em linguagens de programação como Python, SQL e Java. (obrigatório)
Experiência com bibliotecas e frameworks de IA. (preferencial)
Containers (Docker, Kubernetes). (preferencial)
Prompt Engineering. (preferencial)
OpenAI (preferencial)
RAG (preferencial)
Experiência com ferramentas de versionamento de código (Git). (preferencial)
Conhecimento em computação cloud (Azure, Aws). (preferencial)
Conhecimento com ferramentas de CI/CD como GitHub Actions, GOCD ou similares. (preferencial)


Soft Skills


Atitude proativa e dinâmica.
Capacidade de trabalho em equipa e de relacionamento interpessoal.


VALORIZAMOS O TEU IMPACTO

A tua contribuição é importante e será reconhecida. Podes esperar um pacote de remuneração justo e competitivo, que reflete o impacto que crias e o valor que entregas.

Mas sabemos que as recompensas vão muito além dos números.


Oferecemos mais do que um salário. Os nossos benefícios foram concebidos para apoiar a tua vida, os teus objetivos e o teu bem-estar, agora e no futuro.
A tua saúde é a nossa prioridade. Através do BeHealthy, o nosso programa global de bem-estar, promovemos o bem-estar holístico.
Sabemos que a família é tudo. Por isso, oferecemos licença parental, apoio à infância e programas adaptados a cada fase da vida.
Potenciamos as nossas equipas através de oportunidades globais e percursos de carreira diversificados.
Acreditamos no poder da ajuda. Pode dedicar parte do teu tempo de trabalho a atividades de voluntariado.


Estamos aqui para te manter motivado(a), ajudar-te a alcançar os teus objetivos e celebrar o teu progresso em cada etapa do caminho.

CONFORMIDADE LOCAL

O Santander orgulha-se de ser uma organização que promove a igualdade de oportunidades, independentemente da idade, género, deficiência, estado civil, raça, religião ou orientação sexual.

Comprometemo-nos a oferecer um processo de candidatura inclusivo e acessível a todas as pessoas.

O QUE FAZER A SEGUIR

Se consideras que esta função é do teu interesse, candidata-te.

Pronto(a) para dar o próximo passo na tua jornada?

Title: Senior Business intelligence Specialist
Description:
At Efficio, we're not just a consulting firm; we're a team of dedicated experts committed to transforming procurement and supply chain functions. Our mission is to drive sustainable, measurable value for our clients through innovative solutions and deep industry insights. Join us and be part of a dynamic environment where your skills and ideas make a real impact.

Efficio is the world’s largest specialist procurement and supply chain consultancy, with offices across Europe, North America, and the Middle East. We’re a diverse team of over 1,000 individuals, representing more than 60 different nationalities and speaking 40+ languages – and we’re continuing to grow rapidly!

We are recruiting for our Lisbon Office where you'll be joining a growing team of nearly 100 people. Consultants based in Lisbon will support clients globally with the opportunity for international travel. Our consultants provide flexible support to clients whether it is a rapid assessment, short term savings realisation or embedding in a long-term partnership to deliver lasting and measurable value to clients.

This role is ideal for an experienced BI professional who can work independently with senior stakeholders, translate ambiguous business questions into robust reporting solutions, and lead the delivery of high-quality dashboards and analytics products. The successful candidate will bring strong technical experience across BI tooling, SQL, databases, data modelling, and visual analytics, combined with the communication skills needed to influence stakeholders and drive better decision-making through data.

The primary BI platform used in this role will be Sigma, so hands-on Sigma experience is highly desirable. Candidates with strong experience in comparable BI platforms such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or Qlik, and the ability to quickly adapt to Sigma, may also be suitable.

There is also an opportunity to explore the use of n8n, AI agents, and other automation or agentic AI solutions to enhance reporting workflows, generate insights from dashboards, automate recurring analysis, and improve how stakeholders interact with data.

What we are looking for:


5–8 years of experience in business intelligence, analytics, reporting, or data visualization, with strong hands-on experience using Sigma. Experience in Power BI, Tableau, Looker, can be considered.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with databases, data models, joins, KPIs, calculations, and reporting logic.
Proven ability to translate ambiguous business requirements into clear, practical BI solutions, dashboards, and actionable insights.
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, including experience working with senior technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, improve existing reporting processes, and maintain a strong focus on data accuracy, quality, and usability.


Nice to Have:


Experience with n8n, AI agents, workflow automation, or agentic AI solutions.
Familiarity with Python for data analysis, automation, validation, or prototyping.


We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Engineer
HTML:
Excelia is a multinational company in Consulting, Technology, and Professional Services with over 25 years of experience. We provide services in more than 50 countries across Europe, Latin America, and the United States, through our 10 own offices.

Do you have experience as a Data Engineer and are you looking for a new challenge? Then this opportunity is for you!

🔹 Position: Data Engineer

📍 Location: Venda do Pinheiro

Main responsibilities:

  • Design, implement, and maintain scalable data pipelines, ETL/ELT processes, and integrations across multiple systems;
  • Develop, optimize, and maintain database structures, ensuring high data quality, performance, and availability;
  • Work with business stakeholders and analysts to understand data needs and translate them into technical solutions;
  • Support and evolve our data warehouse and reporting environments, particularly in Snowflake and Qlik;
  • Ensure data consistency, accuracy, and integrity across systems and projects;
  • Contribute to improving existing data flows, storage strategies, and analytical frameworks;
  • Collaborate closely with data analysts, software developers, and other IT professionals in an agile environment.

Mandatory requirements:

  • Strong experience with relational databases, particularly PostgreSQL (advanced SQL, performance tuning, schema design);
  • Solid experience with Snowflake (data modeling, ELT patterns, performance optimization);
  • Experience with Qlik (Qlik Sense or QlikView) for data modeling, dashboards, and reporting;
  • Familiarity with data engineering concepts such as ETL/ELT pipelines, data lakes, and data warehouses;
  • Experience with scripting or general-purpose languages (e.g., Python or Java is a plus);
  • Comfortable working in Linux-based environments;
  • Understanding of data governance, data quality, and metadata management;
  • Strong data analysis skills and the ability to translate data into actionable insights;
  • Excellent debugging, troubleshooting, and optimization skills;
  • Good time management skills;
  • Strong sense of ownership and pride in your work and its impact on business success;
  • Team player with strong communication and collaboration skills;
  • Customer/User-centric mindset;
  • English: Fluent

If you believe you are a good fit, don’t hesitate to apply for this opportunity 😉

Title: Reporting, Dados e Qualidade - Teleassistência CVP
HTML:
Descrição da Função

A Teleassistência da Cruz Vermelha pretende combater o isolamento e promover a segurança dos idosos e outros dependentes de natureza diversa, através do toque num botão. Ao pressionar o botão os utentes em situação de solidão, emergência/urgência ou segurança são contactados por um técnico especializado que acionará a melhor resposta. Procuramos um profissional com forte capacidade analítica, orientação para o detalhe e experiência na gestão e tratamento de informação, capaz de assegurar o desenvolvimento de reporting de suporte à decisão. Será igualmente responsável pelo acompanhamento e coordenação dos processos associados aos Sistemas de Gestão da Qualidade, garantindo o cumprimento dos referenciais aplicáveis, a atualização documental e a preparação de auditorias internas e externas. Requisitos:

  • Formação superior em Sistemas de Informação, Análise de Dados, Economia, Engenharia, Gestão, Matemática, Estatística, Informática ou áreas similares;
  • Experiência profissional anterior em funções de análise de dados, reporting ou controlo de qualidade;
  • Formação específica em referenciais e sistemas de certificação da qualidade (ex.: ISO 9001), certificações ou formação complementar em ferramentas de análise e visualização de dados (ex.: Power BI, SQL, Excel Avançado) serão valorizados;
  • Experiência na utilização de ferramentas de reporting e visualização de dados;
  • Experiência em definição e monitorização de KPIs;
  • Conhecimentos de metodologias de qualidade e melhoria contínua;
  • Experiência com ferramentas de automação e tratamento de dados;
  • Experiência em contextos regulados e/ou com grande volume de informação;
  • Boa capacidade de organização e gestão de prioridades;
  • Facilidade de comunicação e trabalho em equipa;
  • Boa capacidade de organização, rigor e atenção ao detalhe;
  • Boa capacidade de resiliência e gestão do stress.
  • Pensamento crítico e orientação para resolução de problemas;
  • Proatividade e autonomia;
  • Rigor técnico e sentido de responsabilidade;
  • Orientação para resultados e melhoria contínua. Principais Responsabilidades:
  • Organizar, definir, recolher, consolidar, validar e analisar dados provenientes das diferentes áreas da TA;
  • Garantir a qualidade, integridade e atualização contínua das bases de dados;
  • Desenvolver dashboards, relatórios analíticos e visualizações de dados para suporte à tomada de decisão;
  • Monitorizar indicadores de desempenho (KPI’s), assegurando a sua atualização e acompanhamento;
  • Produzir análises e relatórios de apoio à gestão, identificando tendências, desvios e oportunidades de melhoria;
  • Desenvolver e otimizar processos de reporting, gestão e auditoria de dados;
  • Realizar análises aos processos operacionais e funcionais, propondo melhorias e ações preventivas;
  • Assegurar a implementação, manutenção e melhoria contínua do Sistema de Gestão da Qualidade (SGQ);
  • Monitorizar o cumprimento dos procedimentos, políticas e documentação do SGQ;
  • Acompanhamento de auditorias internas e externas;
  • Apoiar a coordenação na revisão, monitorização e melhoria do desempenho do SGQ;
  • Elaborar análises de suporte à gestão de fornecedores, clientes e processos críticos do serviço;
  • Apoiar a área de desenvolvimento de projetos e de comunicação nas diferentes na captação, análise, tratamento de Leads;
  • Articular com a pessoa responsável pela área de desenvolvimento de projetos e comunicação, e com a área da Comunicação todo o trabalho relacionado com captação de Leads, elaboração de guiões para maior eficácia na conversão de Leads;
  • Apoiar a Coordenação e as lideranças intermédias da equipa de gestão da TA;
  • Coordenar e supervisionar todo o trabalho diário de manutenção, atualização do Sistema de Gestão de Qualidade;
  • Assegurar, na relação com as pessoas responsáveis pelos diferentes departamentos da equipa da TA, o cumprimento de todos os requisitos da norma, legais e da Cruz Vermelha Portuguesa. Condições oferecidas:
  • Contrato de trabalho sem termo;
  • Integração em equipa dinâmica e colaborativa;
  • Participação em projetos de transformação e análise de dados;
  • Formação contínua e desenvolvimento profissional;
  • Condições salariais compatíveis com a experiência demonstrada. Documentos obrigatórios e candidatura: Deverá enviar para o e-mail abaixo referido os seguintes documentos, com referência “Especialista em Dados, Reporting e Gestão da Qualidade - Teleassistência”:
  • Carta de Motivação;
  • Curriculum Vitae; Com o envio do respetivo CV o candidato declara que aceita o tratamento dos seus dados pessoais aí contidos para fins profissionais relacionados. Os CV não selecionados serão imediatamente destruídos e os CV selecionados serão mantidos na nossa base de dados até ao final do processo. Os dados pessoais recolhidos pela entidade destinam-se apenas ao processo de recrutamento. Localização
  • Lisboa, Portugal

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Quadrante is looking to strengthen its team by hiring a Data Analyst for our Lisbon office.




As a leading engineering and consultancy group with a strong international footprint, Quadrante delivers high-value and innovative solutions across multiple markets. With more than 1.400 professionals worldwide, we are driven by excellence, collaboration, and a strong commitment to the highest professional and ethical standards.




In this role, you will contribute to transforming data into meaningful insights, supporting decision-making processes, and enhancing business performance across the organization.




Responsibilities:

Collect, process, and analyze data across different areas of the Group.
Assess data quality and ensure accuracy, consistency, and reliability of information.
Develop reports and dashboards to support business decision-making.
Continuously improve analytical models and data analysis processes.
Provide data-driven support to different business units, solving problems through data modeling and analytical techniques.




Profile:

Degree in Information Management, Information Systems, Computer Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field.
Up to 3 years of experience in similar roles.
Strong experience in SQL, Python, or equivalent programming languages (mandatory).
Experience with Business Intelligence tools (mandatory).
Advanced knowledge of English (mandatory).
Knowledge of Spanish will be valued.
Strong analytical thinking, communication skills, and team spirit.




What we offer

As part of the Quadrante Group, you will join an ambitious and collaborative team within a growing international organization.

We work every day to deliver high-quality solutions and foster the growth of our people through engaging challenges and diverse projects.

A dynamic and inclusive work environment, where knowledge sharing and teamwork are key.
The opportunity to work with diverse datasets and support multiple business areas.
A role with real impact, contributing directly to strategic decision-making.
A culture that values potential, continuous learning, and professional development.




Join the Quadrante Team!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are growing. And we are looking for talented people.




At Decskill, we believe that technological excellence is driven by human talent.

We are an IT consulting company with more than 10 years of consolidated experience in the market, focused on building long-term relationships with both our clients and our people. Today, we are a community of over 800 professionals, working from Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid, contributing to impactful technology initiatives.

As part of the Astek Group, we combine a strong local culture with a global presence, being active in around 23 countries across 4 continents. This allows us to offer an international context, diverse challenges, and long-term career opportunities, while staying close to our teams.




We are looking for an Data Analyst!




Responsabilities:

Responsible for creating data pipelines in Airflow for data extracting, processing and loading
Responsible for code and query optimization.
Responsible for generating insights and identify trends, anomalies or opportunities to improve users experience and anticipate their daily tasks.
Responsible for delivering data in useful and appealing ways to users.
Responsible for understanding business change and requirement needs, assess the impact and the cost.




Requirements:

Experience with Python.
Experience in creating APIs in Python.
Experience with PySpark.
Experience with SQL, Oracle Data Base.
Experience with Data Warehouses.
Experience with Apache Airflow - Experience in pipeline development.
Experience with Git.
Experience with Agile methodologies.
Good level of English.




Are you looking for an environment that values curiosity and commitment? Here, your contribution has real impact and individual growth is taken seriously.




Find with us the right opportunity to grow!




What you can expect from us

Long-term projects with national and international context
Opportunities to grow technically and professionally
A people-first culture, focused on transparency and trust
Teams that value ownership, collaboration and stability




If you’re interested in this job, please send your CV to cesaltina.abreu@decskill.com with reference “CA/DataAnalyst".




Your next challenge might start here.

Join us! 😊

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Are you ready to make a significant contribution to both saving lives and protecting communities?

At Avincis, we're dedicated to meaningful work with a clear purpose.

We're seeking talented individuals passionate about making a difference while growing their careers in a dynamic environment.

Avincis is one of the world's leading Emergency Aerial Service Operators and the largest in Europe.

We operate from more than 180 bases across Spain, Portugal, Italy, Norway, Sweden, Finland, Greece, Mozambique and Chile.

We operate a fleet of approximately 205 aircraft which includes 165 helicopters and 40 aeroplanes.

Join us and be part of a mission to protect lives, safeguard communities, and make a positive impact.

We are seeking a business-oriented Data Analyst to join our Data & AI team.

Reporting to the Head of Data and AI, this role acts as the bridge between the Data & AI department and the Executive Committee.

You will own the end-to-end data cycle—from gathering executive requirements to transforming them into impactful analytical solutions, dashboards, and reports that drive business decisions.

Responsibilities and activities

Act as the business voice within the Data & AI team

Gather and translate business requirements into technical solutions

Participate in Agile ceremonies and support adoption of agile methodologies

Build and maintain ETL pipelines and support data extraction processes

Develop and deliver high-quality dashboards and reporting solutions

Train business stakeholders on data interpretation and self-service analytics

Ensure data quality, integrity, and compliance with internal standards

Identify opportunities for implementing AI-driven solutions

Core capabilities

Strong analytical mindset with problem-solving skills

Ability to work autonomously in complex data environments

Strong communication and storytelling skills for executive audiences

Collaborative and proactive approach

Experience with Power BI or similar reporting tools

Agile mindset with focus on delivering incremental value

Fluent in English (written and spoken)

Required Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Computer Engineering, or similar

Master's in Business Analytics, Big Data, or related field

Required Experience

At least 12 months of experience in Big Data environments

Experience with Power BI or similar tools

Familiarity with Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, or similar platforms

Knowledge of data governance, data quality, and data security principles

Exposure to AI tools such as Copilot is a plus

Tech stack

Data Platform: Azure Fabric

Cloud tools: Azure DevOps, Azure Boards

Reporting: Power BI

Culture and values

As the aviation sector is highly regulated, safety and compliance are the highest priority.

Avincis is also committed to the highest standards of business ethics with integrity with trust being central values of the organisation.

Every employee in Avincis is expected to be a role model of integrity and high ethical standards.

Our culture is grounded in a shared commitment to excellence, collaboration, and respect.

We believe that how we work is just as important as what we achieve.

Our six core values guide our actions and define who we are:

Safety : We uphold safety as our first priority above all other business outcomes.

Integrity: We are honest, do the right thing, and communicate respectfully and appropriately.

Trust : We foster an environment of openness and reliability, where trust is earned and valued.

Teamwork: We work together and collaborate to achieve collective goals, ensuring that everyone feels welcome in an inclusive and diverse workplace.

Respect : We treat all employees, customers, and stakeholders with dignity, courtesy, and kindness.

We listen to others' ideas and welcome their input to improve.

Accountability : We are answerable for our actions, behaviours, and performance, and we inspire others to uphold their commitments and take responsibility.

Take the next step in your career with Avincis – where meaningful work and personal development converge.

Apply today and be part of something extraordinary!

In accordance with the provisions of Regulation (EU) ******** of 27 April **** (RGPD) and Organic Law ******, of 5 December, on Data Protection and Guarantee of Digital Rights, we inform you and request your consent, or that of your legal representative, to incorporate your data into the files of AVINCIS, as data controller, and to process them in order to carry out your selection process and maintain a comprehensive relationship with you.

The NIF of the Data Controller is A-******** and the address is Aeródromo de Mutxamel, Partida la Almaina, no92, CP: *****, Mutxamel, Alicante.

In this act you give your consent to the transfer of your data, present and future, to those companies and professionals who need your services for the proper completion of the order made by the Data Controller.

You may exercise your rights of access, rectification, cancellation and opposition in the manner and in accordance with the procedures established in the aforementioned Organic Law, by writing to the above address or to the e-mail address ******.

The owner of the data is responsible, in any case, for the truthfulness, accuracy, validity, authenticity and relevance of the Personal Data provided.

AVINCIS will only use the information provided by you for the selection process in which you are involved, or others in which you could be a candidate, unless otherwise indicated, and the signatories undertake to maintain the strictest confidentiality regarding the information obtained inthis process.

Note: As a company committed to equal opportunities between women and men, AVINCIS evaluates all applications according to objective criteria in order to select the most suitable person for the position offered, regardless of gender.

Title: Business Insights Analyst
Description:
This is a remote role.

About Us

Reachdesk is the first truly end-to-end global SaaS gifting and swag platform, helping B2B companies create meaningful connections with prospects, customers, and employees: while driving measurable ROI.

We believe gifting is more than just a transaction: it’s about building genuine human connections that fuel business growth. Whether you’re engaging prospects, celebrating customers, or recognizing employees, our platform makes it easy to deliver personalized gifts, branded swag, and unforgettable event & brand experiences at scale through automated sourcing, storage, and global delivery.

Trusted by many of the most recognized names in technology and SaaS, Reachdesk integrates effortlessly with your marketing, sales, and HRIS tech stack and is powered by a world-class team with hubs in New York, London, and Lisbon.

We’re growing, and we’re currently looking for a Business Insights Analyst to join our Business Systems & Operations team.

The Role

This is a role for someone energised by the connections between how the business sells, how it serves customers, how it runs operationally and makes money, and who wants to use AI to make those connections faster and clearer than ever.

Every function at Reachdesk sees a different slice of the company. You'll be the person who sits across all of them, using available data and reporting outputs from relevant systems, helping leaders apply consistent commercial and operational definitions when interpreting business performance, and helping leaders see the picture none of them could have produced alone.

Your Responsibilities


Partner with leaders across Sales, Customer Success, Marketing, Finance and Operations to understand the decisions they're each trying to make.
Work with available data from commercial, finance and operational systems to produce insights that connect different views of the business.
Use AI to compress synthesis from days into hours, and to keep our framing of key metrics consistent across functions.
Tell the story behind the numbers in a way that lands with whoever's in the room, commercial, financial or operational.
Create lightweight summaries and repeatable insight outputs so teams can keep answering their own follow-on questions.
Experiment with AI-assisted insight workflows, prompt patterns, evals and lightweight automations, and share what works across the team.


About You


2–4 years in an analyst, insights, strategy or consulting role with exposure to more than one function.
Fluency in at least two of: commercial (revenue, retention, GTM), financial (P&L, unit economics, FP&A), operational (throughput, fulfilment, cycle time).
A genuine AI-native working style; you already use LLMs as a core part of how you think, and you're excited to keep pushing.
Strong written and verbal storytelling; you can adapt your framing for a CRO, a CFO or a Head of Operations without missing a beat.
Comfort working with spreadsheets and reporting tools. Working knowledge of SQL is useful but not essential.


Preferred Expertise


B2B SaaS background.
Familiarity with NetSuite, Salesforce, or the corporate gifting and direct mail space.
Light experience with experiment design, attribution, pricing or operational throughput modeling.


Why Join Us


A team that genuinely treats AI as a leverage tool, not a buzzword.
The closest thing in the company to a true cross-functional vantage point; you'll see how every part of Reachdesk fits together.
You’ll work alongside a small, senior Business Systems & Operations team focused on strategic, cross-functional work.


The Benefits


Competitive salary with generous stock options
Flexible PTO that increases the longer you’re with us, plus extra flexibility for life’s unexpected moments
A genuinely collaborative culture with innovative, fun, and ambitious people


Privacy Policy: We process personal information submitted as part of your application in accordance with the Reachdesk Candidate Privacy Notice. Please review Reachdesk’s Privacy Policy here: www.reachdesk.com/candidate-privacy-policy

  • A genuinely collaborative culture with innovative, fun, and ambitious people

Title: Consultor Júnior Data Science & AI
HTML:
Enviar-me por email Guardar

Consultor(a) Júnior Data Science & AI

A Pluto Analytics procura um(a) Consultor(a) Júnior de Data Science & AI para integrar uma equipa focada em resolver problemas reais de negócio com dados e inteligência artificial.

Responsabilidades

  • Participar em projetos de Data Science e Machine Learning de ponta a ponta, desde a preparação de dados até à construção, teste e validação de modelos preditivos, documentando abordagens, hipóteses, resultados e limitações;
  • Desenvolver análises exploratórias, relatórios, dashboards e visualizações para apoiar a tomada de decisão;
  • Trabalhar com dados estruturados e não estruturados, recorrendo a Python, SQL e ferramentas de análise;
  • Colaborar na prototipagem de soluções de IA Generativa, incluindo integração de LLMs, pesquisa semântica, embeddings e pipelines RAG;
  • Apoiar iniciativas de experimentação, avaliação offline/online e testes A/B;
  • Contribuir para soluções de recomendação, personalização, ranking e análise de comportamento do utilizador.

Requisitos

  • Licenciatura ou formação equivalente em Computer Science, Data Science, Machine Learning, Estatística, Matemática, Engenharia ou outra área quantitativa relacionada;
  • Conhecimentos de Python e SQL aplicados a workflows de data science, incluindo manipulação de dados, modelação, experimentação, prototipagem, automação e desenvolvimento de sistemas;
  • Experiência académica, profissional ou prática em projetos de Machine Learning, incluindo preparação de dados, treino, teste e avaliação de modelos, com compreensão dos conceitos fundamentais como features, validação, overfitting e métricas de avaliação, e capacidade de aplicar esses modelos a problemas reais de negócio;
  • Experiência ou contacto com ferramentas de visualização de dados e comunicação de resultados, como Power BI, Tableau ou bibliotecas equivalentes;
  • Forte capacidade analítica e de resolução de problemas;
  • Atenção ao detalhe e compromisso com qualidade;
  • Proatividade, autonomia, curiosidade e vontade de aprender novas tecnologias;
  • Boa capacidade de comunicação, trabalho em equipa e documentação;
  • Capacidade de priorizar, gerir tempo e cumprir prazos, incluindo em contextos remotos ou híbridos;
  • Inglês fluente.

Valorizamos

  • Projetos universitários, estágios, investigação, bootcamps ou projetos pessoais relacionados com Machine Learning, Data Science ou IA Generativa;
  • Conhecimentos de deep learning, NLP, anomaly detection, sentiment analysis ou time series analysis;
  • Experiência com desenvolvimento de chatbots, conversational AI ou sistemas de agent routing;
  • Conhecimento de arquiteturas com RAG, LLMs, embeddings, semantic search e bases de dados vetoriais;
  • Interesse ou experiência prática em recommendation systems, ranking, personalização e análise de comportamento do utilizador, incluindo next best action e desenho de sistemas de recomendação com base em preferências, histórico e feedback;

Perfil que procuramos

Procuramos uma pessoa com base técnica sólida, capacidade de aprendizagem rápida e vontade de trabalhar em problemas reais, alguém que saiba transformar dados e modelos em soluções compreensíveis e úteis para equipas de negócio e tecnologia.

Title: Business Data Analyst
HTML:
Company Description

Inetum is a European leader in digital services. Inetum’s team of 27,000 consultants and specialists strive every day to make a digital impact for businesses, public sector entities and society. Inetum’s solutions aim at contributing to its clients’ performance and innovation as well as the common good. Present in 19 countries with a dense network of sites, Inetum partners with major software publishers to meet the challenges of digital transformation with proximity and flexibility. Driven by its ambition for growth and scale, Inetum generated sales of 2.4 billion euros in 2024.

Job Description

Are you passionate about turning data into actionable insights? At Inetum, we are looking for a Business Data Analyst to bridge the gap between data and business, ensuring that information is available to support strategic decisions efficiently.

Key Responsibilities

  • Gather and define business requirements for data initiatives;
  • Extract and analyze data from commercial and analytical systems for ad hoc analysis;
  • Design and structure data models to create valuable products and ensure data consistency;
  • Perform business analysis to support operations and decision-making;
  • Communicate effectively with stakeholders and translate insights into actionable recommendations;
  • Contribute to the evolution of our Data Warehouse and Datahub.

Deliverables

  • Development of dashboards and data visualization strategies;
  • Creation of documents and reports to support decision-making;
  • Compilation of information and preparation of analytical reports;
  • User Stories for Data Warehouse enhancements;
  • Data modeling within the Data Warehouse;
  • Ad hoc data collection from commercial and analytical systems.

Qualifications

  • Up to 10 years of experience in similar roles;
  • Advanced knowledge of SQL for data extraction and manipulation;
  • Strong experience with dashboards and data visualization tools;
  • Advanced Excel and proficiency in Office tools;
  • Preferably experience with SAS Enterprise Guide and DOMO;
  • Solid understanding of data modeling and warehousing concepts.

Title: Data Analyst – GCP
Description:
CAPGEMINI ENGINEERING

At Capgemini Engineering, the world leader in engineering services, we bring together a global team of engineers, scientists, and architects to help the world’s most innovative companies unleash their potential. From autonomous cars to life‑saving robots, our digital and software technology experts think outside the box as they provide unique R&D and engineering services across all industries. Join us for a career full of opportunities.

Where you can make a difference. Where no two days are the same.




YOUR ROLE

Key Responsibilities

Take ownership of data analysis initiatives, demonstrating a proactive, detail-oriented, and results-driven approach
Analyze, transform, and interpret complex datasets to generate actionable insights and support data-driven business decisions
Collaborate closely with business stakeholders and cross-functional teams to gather requirements and translate them into analytical solutions
Design, build, and maintain efficient queries, data models, and datasets using GCP tools, with a strong focus on BigQuery
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends, patterns, anomalies, and opportunities
Ensure data quality, consistency, and reliability through validation processes, monitoring, documentation, and dataset labeling
Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and KPIs, enabling business users to monitor performance and make informed decisions
Use cloud-based and big data technologies to process and analyze large volumes of structured and semi-structured data
Communicate insights, findings, and recommendations clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Promote a data-driven culture by supporting best practices in analytics, reporting, and data governance




YOUR PROFILE




Experience & Technical Skills

4–6 years of proven experience in a Data Analyst or similar analytics role
Strong proficiency in SQL, with hands-on experience querying large datasets
Experience with Python for data analysis and data preparation (e.g., Pandas, PySpark or equivalent)
Solid hands-on experience with Google Cloud Platform, especially BigQuery
Strong experience building and maintaining interactive dashboards in Power BI, enabling effective data storytelling
Good understanding of data modeling, KPIs, and business metrics
Knowledge of basic statistical concepts (descriptive statistics, trend analysis, hypothesis testing) applied to business analysis
Experience working in large-scale, cloud-based data environments and data warehouses
Familiarity with version control tools (e.g., Git) and data pipelines / CI/CD concepts is a plus
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to explain complex insights clearly and concisely
Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and problem-solving skills
Academic background in Engineering, Data Analytics, Computer Science, Mathematics, or a related field
Mandatory fluency in English




WHAT YOU’LL LOVE ABOUT WORKING HERE

Join a multicultural and inclusive team environment
Enjoy a supportive atmosphere promoting work‑life balance
Hybrid work
Your career growth is central to our mission. Our array of career growth programs and diverse professionals are crafted to support you in exploring a world of opportunities
Access valuable training and certifications in cutting‑edge technologies
Engage in exciting national and international projectsHealth and life insurance
Referral program with bonuses for talent recommendations
Great office locations

Capgemini is an AI‑powered global business and technology transformation partner, delivering tangible business value. We imagine the future of organizations and make it real with AI, technology, and people. With our strong heritage of nearly 60 years, we are a responsible and diverse group of 420,000 team members in more than 50 countries. We deliver end‑to‑end services and solutions with our deep industry expertise and strong partner ecosystem, leveraging our capabilities across strategy, technology, design, engineering, and business operations.

Description:
At Devoteam, we believe that technology with strong human values can actively drive change for the better. Discover how Tech for People unlocks the future, creating a positive impact on the people and the world around us. Join our multidisciplinary team of Cloud experts, Designers, Business consultants, Security experts, Engineers, Developers and other extraordinary talents, spread across more than 20 EMEA countries. Become one of our +10.000 tech and business leaders on cloud, data and cyber security. Let’s fuse creativity with technology together and build innovative solutions that actively change things for the better.

Collect, clean, analyze, and interpret data to generate actionable insights that support strategic and operational decisions

Assess business changes and requirements, evaluating their impact and associated costs
Optimize code, queries, and data processes to improve efficiency and performance
Develop and maintain Airflow pipelines for data extraction, processing, and loading

Title: Data Intelligence
HTML:
We are growing. And we are looking for talented people.


At Decskill, we believe that technological excellence is driven by human talent.

We are an IT consulting company with more than 10 years of consolidated experience in the market, focused on building long-term relationships with both our clients and our people. Today, we are a community of over 800 professionals, working from Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid, contributing to impactful technology initiatives.


As part of the Astek Group, we combine a strong local culture with a global presence, being active in around 23 countries across 4 continents. This allows us to offer an international context, diverse challenges, and long-term career opportunities, while staying close to our teams.


We are looking for a Data Intelligence!


Responsibilities:

  • Contribute to the creation, management and monitoring of the group's CRM strategy and plan, implementation and monitoring;
  • Defining the customer journey and identifying golden moments to talk/relate with the customer;
  • Participate in adhoc and always on Campaigns;
  • Monitor, interpret and analyze KPI's/performance indicators relevant to the business activity;
  • Adapt and create new KPI's based on client requirements and needs;
  • Track various data reports created globally to ensure success and data quality;
  • Update various dashboards and refresh data to ensure proper performance monitoring;
  • Ensure robustness, integrity and quality of data present in multiple databases;
  • Deliver ad hoc data segmentations and extractions for ad hoc analysis and/or for campaign and/or communications purposes.


Requirements:

  • Previous experience up to 2 years in CRM or Data Intelligence;
  • Be digital native and passionate about digital technology and communications;
  • Analyze and monitor digital performance dashboards;
  • Master all digital channels in all their aspects (content/storytelling, audiences, media and performance);
  • Have proven experience in using Google Analytics, MS and Business Intelligence tools (DataStudio, Spotfire, MS Power Bi, MS Office) and general knowledge of Adobe;
  • Be able to anticipate and be able to identify the right priorities;
  • Ability to make decisions and take action/Problem solving;
  • Critical thinking, constantly evolving;
  • Good level of spoken and written English.


Are you looking for an environment that values curiosity and commitment? Here, your contribution has real impact and individual growth is taken seriously.


Find with us the right opportunity to grow!


What you can expect from us:

  • Long-term projects with national and international context (if applicable)
  • Opportunities to grow technically and professionally
  • A people-first culture, focused on transparency and trust
  • Teams that value ownership, collaboration and stability.


If you’re interested in this job, please send your CV to career@decskill.com with reference “CA/DataIntelligence”.


Thank You! :)


Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being.

Title: Data Analyst (m/f/d)
Description:
Your new company

You will be joining a globally recognised organisation operating within the transport and logistics sector, known for its strong international footprint and complex operational network. With activities spanning multiple continents, the company plays a key role in enabling global trade and supply chain efficiency. Focused on digital transformation and data-driven decision-making, it invests heavily in technology, analytics, and innovation to optimise performance and deliver high-quality services to its customers.




Your new role

As a Data Analyst, you will be part of a dynamic Business Intelligence team responsible for transforming data into meaningful insights. Acting as a trusted partner to various business units, you will support decision-making processes through advanced reporting, data analysis, and automation initiatives. You will work closely with stakeholders across operations, finance, and IT to ensure data accuracy, optimise reporting processes, and continuously enhance BI solutions.




Responsibilities

Design, develop and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI
Gather, clean and transform data from multiple sources to ensure quality and consistency
Monitor key performance indicators and deliver actionable insights to business stakeholders
Build and optimise data models, dataflows and queries to support reporting needs
Automate recurring processes using Power Automate and Python
Analyse large datasets to identify trends, inefficiencies and improvement opportunities
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand business requirements and translate them into technical solutions
Support ongoing enhancement of BI tools and reporting frameworks
Ensure proper documentation and adherence to data governance standards
Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives within the BI and analytics landscape




What you'll need to succeed

Degree in Data Analytics, Engineering, Computer Science or a related field
1–2 years of experience in a similar Data Analyst or BI role
Strong knowledge of Power BI (dashboards, data modelling, Power Query)
Experience with Power Automate and Python for data processing and automation
Solid analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex data sets
Fluency in English (mandatory)
Previous experience within transport or logistics environments (preferred)
Strong problem-solving mindset and attention to detail
Ability to communicate insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders




What you'll get in return

You will join an international and fast-paced environment where data plays a central role in business success. The company offers excellent working conditions, a collaborative atmosphere and opportunities for professional growth.

Health insurance for employee and children, plus medical assistance at home
Flexible working environment with hybrid model (3 days on-site, 2 remote)
Working hours: 07:30 – 16:30, Monday to Friday (40 hours/week)
Exposure to complex, international data projects
Continuous learning and development opportunities




What you need to do now

If you are looking to develop your career in a data-driven environment and work on impactful BI initiatives, this could be the right opportunity for you. Apply now!

Title: Data Engineer Junior
Description:
Sobre a Welvaart

Na Welvaart, criamos soluções tecnológicas centradas nas pessoas.

A nossa liderança próxima e cultura de evolução flexível potenciam o talento das equipas e elevam a qualidade das entregas.

Combinamos rigor, inovação e empatia para impulsionar projetos que transformam negócios e constroem relações de confiança duradouras.

Complementamos esta visão com uma oferta de Marketing Digital focada em performance, ajudando empresas a ganhar visibilidade, fortalecer a presença online e acelerar o crescimento com estratégias inteligentes e resultados mensuráveis.

Projeto

Procuramos um Data Engineer Júnior para integrar uma equipa de Business Intelligence com forte componente operacional. Esta função é essencial para sustentar a operação diária da área de BI, garantindo a gestão, processamento e disponibilização de dados transacionais. A equipa é responsável pelo tratamento de carteiras de clientes e identificação de oportunidades de atuação, sendo também o ponto central de inserção e gestão de dados.

Função


Assegurar o suporte à operação diária da equipa de BI
Processar e integrar dados transacionais provenientes de diferentes fontes
Inserir, validar e manter dados em sistemas internos
Trabalhar carteiras de clientes, identificando aqueles com maior potencial de atuação
Desenvolver e manter pipelines de dados
Criar e suportar relatórios operacionais (Reporting Services e Power BI)
Colaborar na evolução da arquitetura de dados, incluindo implementação de CI/CD
Trabalhar com ferramentas de integração de dados (Integration Services)
Participar em iniciativas futuras com Airflow e Python



Estamos à procura de


Formação superior em Engenharia Informática, Sistemas de Informação ou similar
Experiência mínima (académica ou profissional) em:
SQL (obrigatório)
Python (obrigatório)
Conhecimentos em ferramentas Microsoft:
Power BI
SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS)
SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS)
Interesse em engenharia de dados e automação
Capacidade de trabalhar num ambiente operacional e orientado a detalhe

HTML:
Sobre a Welvaart

Na Welvaart, criamos soluções tecnológicas centradas nas pessoas.

A nossa liderança próxima e cultura de evolução flexível potenciam o talento das equipas e elevam a qualidade das entregas.

Combinamos rigor, inovação e empatia para impulsionar projetos que transformam negócios e constroem relações de confiança duradouras.

Complementamos esta visão com uma oferta de Marketing Digital focada em performance, ajudando empresas a ganhar visibilidade, fortalecer a presença online e acelerar o crescimento com estratégias inteligentes e resultados mensuráveis.

Projeto

Procuramos um Data Engineer Júnior para integrar uma equipa de Business Intelligence com forte componente operacional. Esta função é essencial para sustentar a operação diária da área de BI, garantindo a gestão, processamento e disponibilização de dados transacionais. A equipa é responsável pelo tratamento de carteiras de clientes e identificação de oportunidades de atuação, sendo também o ponto central de inserção e gestão de dados.

Função

  • Assegurar o suporte à operação diária da equipa de BI
  • Processar e integrar dados transacionais provenientes de diferentes fontes
  • Inserir, validar e manter dados em sistemas internos
  • Trabalhar carteiras de clientes, identificando aqueles com maior potencial de atuação
  • Desenvolver e manter pipelines de dados
  • Criar e suportar relatórios operacionais (Reporting Services e Power BI)
  • Colaborar na evolução da arquitetura de dados, incluindo implementação de CI/CD
  • Trabalhar com ferramentas de integração de dados (Integration Services)
  • Participar em iniciativas futuras com Airflow e Python


Estamos à procura de

  • Formação superior em Engenharia Informática, Sistemas de Informação ou similar
  • Experiência mínima (académica ou profissional) em:
  • SQL (obrigatório)
  • Python (obrigatório)
  • Conhecimentos em ferramentas Microsoft:
  • Power BI
  • SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS)
  • SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS)
  • Interesse em engenharia de dados e automação
  • Capacidade de trabalhar num ambiente operacional e orientado a detalhe
  • Conhecimentos em práticas de CI/CD
  • Experiência ou interesse em Apache Airflow
  • Conhecimento geral de arquiteturas de dados
  • Híbrido: 2 vezes em Lisboa
  • Início projeto: 1 agosto 2026


O que pode descobrir connosco?

  • Fazer parte de uma start-up tecnológica em crescimento

  • Estrutura de remuneração justa e equitativa (Consultant Profile)
  • Formação e certificação
  • Gestão do percurso profissional
  • Mais de 30 parcerias

Title: Data Analyst II
Description:
About Us

GoCardless is a global bank payment company. Over 100,000 businesses, from start-ups to household names, use GoCardless to collect and send payments through direct debit, real-time payments and open banking.

GoCardless processes US$130bn+ of payments annually, across 30+ countries; helping customers collect and send both recurring and one-off payments, without the chasing, stress or expensive fees. We use AI-powered solutions to improve payment success and reduce fraud. And, with open banking connectivity to over 2,500 banks, we help our customers make faster, more informed decisions.

We are headquartered in the UK with offices in London and Leeds, and additional locations in Australia, France, Ireland, Latvia, Portugal and the United States.

At GoCardless, we're all about supporting you! We’re committed to making our hiring process inclusive and accessible. If you need extra support or adjustments, reach out to your Talent Partner — we’re here to help!

And remember: we don’t expect you to meet every single requirement. If you’re excited by this role, we encourage you to apply!

Payment Intelligence

Our mission is to make GoCardless the best way to pay and be paid. Our ambition is to dramatically improve payment performance and safeguard our customers from bad outcomes like payment fraud and failures. You will help find data opportunities and areas to improve our payment intelligence products. You should be comfortable with large data sets, customer data, and machine learning principles. You’ll work alongside Product Managers, Engineers and Data Scientists in our two core Payment Intelligence teams to provide critical insights and help drive product direction.

Our existing Payment Intelligence Products are already one of the key reasons why customers choose GoCardless. These include Protect+, which defends customers from fraud, and Success+, which helps customers intelligently recover failed payments. You will need to own our understanding of these products and identify insights and opportunities to help us develop new intelligent features.

What You'll Do

At GoCardless analytics is at the core of everything we do and drives business performance through data insight. You will play a critical role partnering closely with stakeholders across the business (including product, operations, marketing, and commercial teams) in order to deliver actionable insights to influence decisions at every level of the organisation. The role is focussed on identifying value and driving change throughout the business derived from insights. Your insights will be turned into actions that will help drive the future of GoCardless. You will act as the bridge between the data, technical and business stakeholders informing strategic plans and will own and evolve a suite of reports designed to give a wide range of stakeholders access insights.


Own and iterate on experiments to generate user insight and drive business metrics, allowing the team to quickly take action on business opportunities.
Define how we quantitatively evaluate success, setting KPIs, designing tracking to measure what really matters, delving into the leading indicators of user behaviour
Influence the organisation to increase Product Data coverage and implement user tracking best practices - acting as a mentor to guide other members of the organisation to become data driven
Build models of user-segmentation, customer lifetime value etc in order to inform strategic product & commercial decisions
Continuously innovate on the practices and the tools we rely on to deliver the right insights at the right decision points (A/B Testing, Analysis (SQL), Visualisation (Looker), Tracking (Amplitude), Automation (Python), AI (Gemini)


What Excites You


Become a strategic partner and advisor for the business: you like to proactively seek out gaps in our product capabilities, suggest areas of improvement based on tangible data, as well as owning processes to run and improve testing from the tracking point of view when developing new features.
Act as a catalyst for change across the organization, driving the evolution of our data analytics standards, internal processes, and overall product data strategy to ensure scalability and operational excellence.
Translate complex technical findings into compelling, high-impact narratives. You will bridge the gap between technical data and commercial strategy, influencing product roadmaps and executive decision-making across the entire organization.
Stay current with AI and data trends to solve real-world problems. You like to leverage these technologies to enhance the payment experience, as well as to optimize data analysis, and deliver high-impact insights and strategic recommendations across the business.
Unlocking new layers of payment performance, from uncovering new fraud vectors to maximizing success rates
Serve as a cross-functional mentor, championing a data-driven mindset and elevating the analytical capabilities of team members throughout the company.


What Excites Us


You have a background working in similar fast paced product-driven teams and are great at communicating insights to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders, to drive data-informed product decisions
You are a self-starter, focused on results, with a demonstrated background in using analytics to drive the understanding, growth, and success of a product
You have extensive experience with data visualisation tools mentioned above and develop compelling visualisations which influence decision making and business understanding
You have advanced SQL skills and intermediate Python skills & have experience leveraging AI capabilities to enhance data analysis, automate tasks, and improve decision-making processes
You have a background working in similar fast paced teams and are great at communicating insights to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders, to drive data-informed decisions.
You are solution-oriented and able to own projects from start to finish. You are comfortable motivating stakeholders across the business and negotiating timelines to unblock yourself and make things happen!


Salary range: the expected base salary range for this position is between €32,000 - €48,000.

Base salary ranges are based on role, job level, location, and market data. Please note that whilst we strive to offer competitive compensation, our approach is to pay between the minimum and the mid-point of the pay range until performance can be assessed in role. Offers will take into account level of experience, interview assessment, budgets and parity between you and fellow employees at GoCardless doing similar work.

(some of) The good stuff


Wellbeing - stay healthy with dedicated support and medical cover
Work away scheme - gives you the option to work away from your country of residence for up to 90 days in any 12 month period
Adaptive Working - allows you to work flexibly, around your lifestyle
Equity - all permanently employed GCs get equity to help you make a valuable contribution
Parental leave - to suit everyone embarking on life's great adventure
Learning Budget - lead your own development with an annual learning budget
Time off - generous holiday allowance, + 3 annual volunteer days, + 4 annual business-wide wellness days (‘GC Fridays’)


Life at GoCardless

We're an organisation defined by our values; We start with why before we begin any project, to ensure it’s aligned with our mission. We act with integrity, always. We care deeply about what we do and we know it's essential that we be humble whilst we do it. Working this way creates the GC magic- the reason we all love showing up to work.

Diversity & Inclusion

As of April 2025, we had 806 employees (GeeCees) globally, with 524 based in the UK, 163 based in Latvia and 119 across our other offices.

To Ensure That We're Representative Of The World Around Us - And To Be Able To Review Relevant Benchmarks - We Ask GeeCees To Voluntarily Disclose Diversity Data. This Year, The Proportion Of GeeCees Providing Data Increased To 88% (up From 79% In 2024). With Regards To Diversity Within GoCardless, We Can See GeeCees Identifying As

Asian, Black, Mixed or Other — 25%

Neurodiverse — 9%

LGBTQIA+ — 9%

Disabled — 1%

Average age — 33

We’re rooting for you during your application and GoCardless aims to provide reasonable adjustments to make our recruitment process as remarkable and accessible as we can. Please speak to your Talent Partner if you need extra support.

If you want to learn more, you can read about our Employee Resource Groups and objectives here as well as our latest D&I Report

Sustainability

We’re committed to reducing our impact on the environment, leaving a more sustainable world for future generations. In 2021 we became co-founders of the Tech Zero coalition, a group of businesses committed to taking climate action as part of the UNFCCC Race to Zero. We aim to reduce our impact and to create positive change on the natural world. Check out our sustainability action plan here.

Find out more about Life at GoCardless via Twitter, Instagram and LinkedIn.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Marley Spoon

We’re Marley Spoon, a food-tech company on a mission to make amazing, home-cooked meals easy and sustainable. Since 2014, we’ve grown into a global meal kit platform, delivering flexible, personalized recipes to customers across six countries.

Behind every box is a network of intelligent systems, built by lean, cross-functional teams who care deeply about customer experience, sustainability and quality. Our Data Tribe plays a key role in making that happen – one insight, one experiment, and one decision at a time.

About The Role

As a Data Analyst, you’ll turn complex customer and growth data into clear insights that guide decisions across Marketing, Product, and Commercial teams. You’ll design analyses, build dashboards, and support experiments that help us understand what drives acquisition, conversion, retention, and long-term customer value.

You’ll have the chance to go beyond reporting: working on high-impact business questions, evaluating performance trends, and helping stakeholders understand what is truly moving the needle. This is a hands-on role with real ownership, where your work will be seen, used, and acted on.

What You’ll Do

In this role, you will:


Design, analyze, and interpret A/B tests and quasi-experiments to inform marketing and growth decisions.
Build and maintain reliable dashboards and data models in Looker to support self-serve analytics across the business.
Explore customer behaviour across acquisition, retention, engagement, and lifecycle performance using data from Snowflake, Amplitude, and other sources.
Investigate changes in growth, conversion, and commercial performance to identify what is driving impact.
Work closely with Marketers, Product Managers, and cross-functional stakeholders to frame business questions, choose the right analytical approach, and communicate results clearly.
Share findings in a simple, visual way so stakeholders can act quickly and confidently.


About You

You’re curious, analytical, and commercially aware. You enjoy turning open questions into structured analysis, and you care about making your work understandable and actionable.

Must-haves


Around 3+ years of experience in data analytics, ideally in marketing, growth, product, e-commerce, or a similar business environment.
Confidence with SQL, including joining tables, building aggregations, and writing efficient queries.
Experience with BI / data visualisation tools (Looker preferred, but similar tools are fine).
Hands-on experience working with data warehouses (e.g. Snowflake or similar).
Practical experience with experimentation (A/B tests and/or quasi-experimental methods), either designing, analysing, or interpreting tests.
Clear communication skills – you can explain complex concepts in a simple, visual, and structured way.
Comfortable working in English in a distributed team.


Nice-to-haves


Familiarity with Python or similar tools for deeper analysis.
Experience with Amplitude or other product analytics tools.
Previous experience in subscription, marketplace, DTC, or other data-rich consumer businesses.
Experience with cohort analysis, lifecycle analytics, funnel performance, or behavioural segmentation.


If you don’t meet every single point but feel you can grow into this role, we’d still be happy to hear from you.

How We Work – Location, Remote & Collaboration


This role is remote within Portugal – we can hire you anywhere in PT, as long as you have the right to work here.
We have a Lisbon office where you’re welcome to join collaboration days, workshops or social events, but regular office attendance is optional.
We collaborate mainly during European business hours, with flexibility to organise your day in a way that works for you and your team.


Benefits


Remote with optional office days.
22 annual leave days +2 days extra days for every year of tenure (up to 6).
5 training days per year.
Private health insurance provided by Tranquilidade.
Food allowance of 7.62€/worked day under by Coverflex.
24/7 confidential employee assistance program.

Title: Assistente Dentária
HTML:
Lisboa

 Contrato a Termo Incerto

Terá como principais responsabilidades:

  • Atendimento e acompanhamento dos clientes aos consultórios;
  • Garantir a manutenção do gabinete (material clínico e administrativo);
  • Garantir o apoio no decorrer da consulta, quando solicitado;
  • Identificar exames/tratamentos feitos no decorrer da consulta e documentação respetiva e entregar na receção administrativa;
  • Encaminhar o cliente para o check-out administrativo;
  • Fazer atendimento telefónico interno e transmitir informações ao cliente/médico.



Perfil:

  • Curso de Assistente Dentária;
  • 12º Ano de escolaridade;
  • Experiência profissional (1 a 2 anos);
  • Capacidade de organização;
  • Disponibilidade de horários;
  • Gosto pelo contacto com clientes.



Oferecemos:

  • Integração num dos maiores grupos privados de prestação de cuidados de saúde em Portugal;
  • Possibilidade de evolução de carreira;
  • Percurso formativo na área da especialidade;
  • Seguro de Saúde;
  • Plano de Saúde para Colaboradores (que inclui familiares em condições favoráveis).



No caso de reunir os requisitos para a função, agradecemos que envie a sua candidatura.

Title: Gestor/a de Ciência - Dados de Investigação
HTML:
A FCCN, serviços digitais da FCT - Fundação para a Ciência e a Tecnologia, tem como objetivo dotar as comunidades académica e de investigação portuguesas dos meios tecnológicos necessários à produção de ciência e conhecimento de excelência, promovendo o acesso e a partilha do conhecimento científico. É com este propósito que a FCCN atua diariamente, assegurando Tecnologia para o Conhecimento.

Na Área do Conhecimento Científico da FCCN, trabalhamos para garantir o acesso da comunidade académica e científica a informação de elevada qualidade, promover o acesso aberto à produção científica nacional e facilitar a gestão da informação sobre ciência e tecnologia em Portugal.

Para nos ajudar nesta missão pretendemos reforçar a nossa equipa com um/a Gestor/a de Ciência que irá desenvolver as suas atividades no âmbito da iniciativa POLEN que tem como objetivo dar resposta às necessidades da comunidade científica e de ensino na área de gestão de dados de investigação.

Na FCCN, acreditamos que a igualdade de género é fundamental para criar um ambiente de trabalho inclusivo e inovador. Comprometemo-nos a promover oportunidades iguais para todas as pessoas, independentemente de género, identidade ou expressão, assegurando que cada candidatura é avaliada de forma justa e transparente.

Junta-te a nós para construirmos uma equipa diversa, onde o talento e as ideias de todos/as são valorizados e respeitados.

Funções

  • Apoiar a coordenação, análise e suporte da correta execução do plano de atividades e do orçamento;
  • Apoiar, em articulação com a equipa, a realização de estudos técnicos, análises funcionais, levantamento de requisitos, controlo da implementação e testes;
  • Apoiar, em conjunto com uma equipa multidisciplinar, processos de contratação pública com fornecedores;
  • Monitorizar as condições resultantes de contratos celebrados com fornecedores;
  • Apoiar a gestão e operação de serviços digitais de apoio na área da ciência aberta e dados abertos de investigação como: serviço de planos de gestão de dados e serviço de sincronização e partilha de dados;
  • Assegurar, em conjunto com outros elementos da equipa, funções de representação e comunicação junto de parceiros nacionais e internacionais

Requirements

  • Formação superior;
  • Conhecimento do ecossistema de investigação e ensino superior;
  • Conhecimento e experiência em gestão e coordenação de projetos, incluindo as componentes de planeamento, monitorização e avaliação;
  • Participação na produção de estudos e relatórios técnicos;
  • Capacidade de análise de informação e sentido crítico;
  • Experiência no acompanhamento e participação ativa em projetos distribuídos por várias equipas e stakeholders (instituições, fornecedores);
  • Boa capacidade de comunicação e de relacionamento interpessoal;
  • Iniciativa e autonomia;
  • Fluência no português e inglês, escrito e falado, nomeadamente capacidade para elaborar relatórios e realizar apresentações em ambos os idiomas

Competências comportamentais

  • Organização, método e disciplina;
  • Proatividade, autonomia e orientação para resultados;
  • Capacidade de trabalho em equipa e comunicação funcional;
  • Capacidade de organização, planeamento e gestão de prioridades;
  • Espírito crítico, rigor e atenção ao detalhe

Outras competências e conhecimentos valorizados

  • Conhecimentos no campo da ciência aberta, em particular na vertente de dados abertos de investigação;
  • Experiência na elaboração de documentação técnica, incluindo requisitos de negócio, para implementação por equipas de desenvolvimento de IT;
  • Experiência em atividades de comunicação, disseminação e formação

Benefits

  • Integração numa equipa jovem e dinâmica com ambiente descontraído, onde valorizamos a proximidade e o espírito de equipa;
  • Regime de trabalho híbrido;
  • Formação contínua com vista ao teu desenvolvimento profissional;
  • Acesso a um Campus com estacionamento gratuito, acolhedor e bem localizado em Lisboa;
  • Várias iniciativas internas para promover o convívio e interação entre colaboradores/as;
  • Possibilidade de adesão à ADSE;
  • Refeitório;
  • Contrato de trabalho a termo até 31/12/2026;
  • Gestão por objetivos
